9_17_1.ESV: Now the Philistines gathered their armies for battle. And they were gathered at Socoh, which belongs to Judah, and encamped between Socoh and Azekah, in Ephes-dammim.
9_17_1.NASB: Now the Philistines gathered their armies for battle; and they were gathered at Socoh which belongs to Judah, and they camped between Socoh and Azekah, in Ephes-dammim.
9_17_1.NLT: The Philistines now mustered their army for battle and camped between Socoh in Judah and Azekah at Ephes-dammim.
9_17_1.WEB: Now the Philistines gathered together their armies to battle; and they were gathered together at Socoh, which belongs to Judah, and encamped between Socoh and Azekah, in Ephesdammim.
9_17_1.CSB17: The Philistines gathered their forces for war at Socoh in Judah and camped between Socoh and Azekah in Ephes-dammim.
9_17_1.AMP: Now the Philistines gathered their armies for battle and were assembled at Socoh, which belongs to Judah; and they camped between Socoh and Azekah, in Ephes-dammim.
9_17_1.MSG: The Philistines drew up their troops for battle. They deployed them at Socoh in Judah, and set up camp between Socoh and Azekah at Ephes Dammim.
9_17_1.NIV: David and GoliathNow the Philistines gathered their forces for war and assembled at Socoh in Judah. They pitched camp at Ephes Dammim, between Socoh and Azekah.
9_17_1.NRSVCE: Now the Philistines gathered their armies for battle; they were gathered at Socoh, which belongs to Judah, and encamped between Socoh and Azekah, in Ephes-dammim.

9_17_2.NKJV: And Saul and the men of Israel were gathered together, and they encamped in the Valley of Elah, and drew up in battle array against the Philistines.
9_17_2.KJV: And Saul and the men of Israel were gathered together, and pitched by the valley of Elah, and set the battle in array against the Philistines. set: Heb. ranged the battle
9_17_2.ESV: And Saul and the men of Israel were gathered, and encamped in the Valley of Elah, and drew up in line of battle against the Philistines.
9_17_2.NASB: Saul and the men of Israel were gathered and camped in the valley of Elah, and drew up in battle array to encounter the Philistines.
9_17_2.NLT: Saul countered by gathering his Israelite troops near the valley of Elah.
9_17_2.WEB: Saul and the men of Israel were gathered together, and encamped in the valley of Elah, and set the battle in array against the Philistines.
9_17_2.CSB17: Saul and the men of Israel gathered and camped in the Valley of Elah; then they lined up in battle formation to face the Philistines.
9_17_2.AMP: Saul and the men of Israel were gathered together and they camped in the Valley of Elah, and assembled in battle formation to meet the Philistines.
9_17_2.MSG: Saul and the Israelites came together, camped at Oak Valley, and spread out their troops in battle readiness for the Philistines.
9_17_2.NIV: Saul and the Israelites assembled and camped in the Valley of Elah and drew up their battle line to meet the Philistines.
9_17_2.NRSVCE: Saul and the Israelites gathered and encamped in the valley of Elah, and formed ranks against the Philistines.

9_17_3.NKJV: The Philistines stood on a mountain on one side, and Israel stood on a mountain on the other side, with a valley between them.
9_17_3.KJV: And the Philistines stood on a mountain on the one side, and Israel stood on a mountain on the other side: and there was a valley between them.
9_17_3.ESV: And the Philistines stood on the mountain on the one side, and Israel stood on the mountain on the other side, with a valley between them.
9_17_3.NASB: The Philistines stood on the mountain on one side while Israel stood on the mountain on the other side, with the valley between them.
9_17_3.NLT: So the Philistines and Israelites faced each other on opposite hills, with the valley between them.
9_17_3.WEB: The Philistines stood on the mountain on the one side, and Israel stood on the mountain on the other side: and there was a valley between them.
9_17_3.CSB17: The Philistines were standing on one hill, and the Israelites were standing on another hill with a ravine between them.
9_17_3.AMP: The Philistines were standing on the mountain on one side and Israel was standing on the mountain on the other side, with the valley between them.
9_17_3.MSG: The Philistines were on one hill, the Israelites on the opposing hill, with the valley between them.
9_17_3.NIV: The Philistines occupied one hill and the Israelites another, with the valley between them.
9_17_3.NRSVCE: The Philistines stood on the mountain on the one side, and Israel stood on the mountain on the other side, with a valley between them.

9_17_4.NKJV: And a champion went out from the camp of the Philistines, named Goliath, from Gath, whose height was six cubits and a span.
9_17_4.KJV: And there went out a champion out of the camp of the Philistines, named Goliath, of Gath, whose height was six cubits and a span.
9_17_4.ESV: And there came out from the camp of the Philistines a champion named Goliath of Gath, whose height was six cubits and a span.
9_17_4.NASB: Then a champion came out from the armies of the Philistines named Goliath, from Gath, whose height was six cubits and a span.
9_17_4.NLT: Then Goliath, a Philistine champion from Gath, came out of the Philistine ranks to face the forces of Israel. He was over nine feet tall!
9_17_4.WEB: A champion out of the camp of the Philistines named Goliath, of Gath, whose height was six cubits and a span went out.
9_17_4.CSB17: Then a champion named Goliath, from Gath, came out from the Philistine camp. He was nine feet, nine inches tall
9_17_4.AMP: Then a champion came out from the camp of the Philistines named Goliath of Gath, whose height was six cubits and a span.
9_17_4.MSG: A giant nearly ten feet tall stepped out from the Philistine line into the open, Goliath from Gath.
9_17_4.NIV: A champion named Goliath, who was from Gath, came out of the Philistine camp. He was over nine feet tall.
9_17_4.NRSVCE: And there came out from the camp of the Philistines a champion named Goliath, of Gath, whose height was six cubits and a span.

9_17_5.NKJV: He had a bronze helmet on his head, and he was armed with a coat of mail, and the weight of the coat was five thousand shekels of bronze.
9_17_5.KJV: And he had an helmet of brass upon his head, and he was armed with a coat of mail; and the weight of the coat was five thousand shekels of brass. armed: Heb. clothed
9_17_5.ESV: He had a helmet of bronze on his head, and he was armed with a coat of mail, and the weight of the coat was five thousand shekels of bronze.
9_17_5.NASB: [He had] a bronze helmet on his head, and he was clothed with scale-armor which weighed five thousand shekels of bronze.
9_17_5.NLT: He wore a bronze helmet, and his bronze coat of mail weighed 125 pounds.
9_17_5.WEB: He had a helmet of brass on his head, and he wore a coat of mail; and the weight of the coat was five thousand shekels of brass.
9_17_5.CSB17: and wore a bronze helmet and bronze scale armor that weighed one hundred twenty-five pounds.
9_17_5.AMP: He had a bronze helmet on his head, and wore a coat of scale-armor (overlapping metal plates) which weighed5,000 shekels of bronze.
9_17_5.MSG: He had a bronze helmet on his head and was dressed in armor—126 pounds of it!
9_17_5.NIV: He had a bronze helmet on his head and wore a coat of scale armor of bronze weighing five thousand shekels;
9_17_5.NRSVCE: He had a helmet of bronze on his head, and he was armed with a coat of mail; the weight of the coat was five thousand shekels of bronze.

9_17_6.NKJV: And he had bronze armor on his legs and a bronze javelin between his shoulders.
9_17_6.KJV: And he had greaves of brass upon his legs, and a target of brass between his shoulders. target: or, gorget
9_17_6.ESV: And he had bronze armor on his legs, and a javelin of bronze slung between his shoulders.
9_17_6.NASB: [He] also [had] bronze greaves on his legs and a bronze javelin [slung] between his shoulders.
9_17_6.NLT: He also wore bronze leg armor, and he carried a bronze javelin on his shoulder.
9_17_6.WEB: He had brass shin armor on his legs, and a brass javelin between his shoulders.
9_17_6.CSB17: There was bronze armor on his shins, and a bronze javelin was slung between his shoulders.
9_17_6.AMP: He had bronze shin protectors on his legs and a bronze javelin hung between his shoulders.
9_17_6.MSG: He wore bronze shin guards and carried a bronze sword.
9_17_6.NIV: on his legs he wore bronze greaves, and a bronze javelin was slung on his back.
9_17_6.NRSVCE: He had greaves of bronze on his legs and a javelin of bronze slung between his shoulders.

9_17_7.NKJV: Now the staff of his spear was like a weaver’s beam, and his iron spearhead weighed six hundred shekels; and a shield-bearer went before him.
9_17_7.KJV: And the staff of his spear was like a weaver's beam; and his spear's head weighed six hundred shekels of iron: and one bearing a shield went before him.
9_17_7.ESV: The shaft of his spear was like a weaver's beam, and his spear's head weighed six hundred shekels of iron. And his shield-bearer went before him.
9_17_7.NASB: The shaft of his spear was like a weaver's beam, and the head of his spear [weighed] six hundred shekels of iron; his shield-carrier also walked before him.
9_17_7.NLT: The shaft of his spear was as heavy and thick as a weaver’s beam, tipped with an iron spearhead that weighed 15 pounds. His armor bearer walked ahead of him carrying a shield.
9_17_7.WEB: The staff of his spear was like a weaver’s beam; and his spear’s head weighed six hundred shekels of iron. His shield bearer went before him.
9_17_7.CSB17: His spear shaft was like a weaver’s beam, and the iron point of his spear weighed fifteen pounds. In addition, a shield-bearer was walking in front of him.
9_17_7.AMP: The [wooden] shaft of his spear was like a weaver's beam; the blade-head of his spear weighed six hundred shekels of iron. And a shield-bearer walked in front of him.
9_17_7.MSG: His spear was like a fence rail—the spear tip alone weighed over fifteen pounds. His shield bearer walked ahead of him.
9_17_7.NIV: His spear shaft was like a weaver’s rod, and its iron point weighed six hundred shekels. His shield bearer went ahead of him.
9_17_7.NRSVCE: The shaft of his spear was like a weaver’s beam, and his spear’s head weighed six hundred shekels of iron; and his shield-bearer went before him.

9_17_8.NKJV: Then he stood and cried out to the armies of Israel, and said to them, “Why have you come out to line up for battle? Am I not a Philistine, and you the servants of Saul? Choose a man for yourselves, and let him come down to me.
9_17_8.KJV: And he stood and cried unto the armies of Israel, and said unto them, Why are ye come out to set your battle in array? am not I a Philistine, and ye servants to Saul? choose you a man for you, and let him come down to me.
9_17_8.ESV: He stood and shouted to the ranks of Israel, “Why have you come out to draw up for battle? Am I not a Philistine, and are you not servants of Saul? Choose a man for yourselves, and let him come down to me.
9_17_8.NASB: He stood and shouted to the ranks of Israel and said to them, 'Why do you come out to draw up in battle array? Am I not the Philistine and you servants of Saul? Choose a man for yourselves and let him come down to me.
9_17_8.NLT: Goliath stood and shouted a taunt across to the Israelites. “Why are you all coming out to fight?” he called. “I am the Philistine champion, but you are only the servants of Saul. Choose one man to come down here and fight me!
9_17_8.WEB: He stood and cried to the armies of Israel, and said to them, “Why have you come out to set your battle in array? Am I not a Philistine, and you servants to Saul? Choose a man for yourselves, and let him come down to me.
9_17_8.CSB17: He stood and shouted to the Israelite battle formations: “Why do you come out to line up in battle formation?” He asked them, “Am I not a Philistine and are you not servants of Saul? Choose one of your men and have him come down against me.
9_17_8.AMP: Goliath stood and shouted to the battle lines of Israel, saying to them, "Why have you come out to draw up for battle? Am I not the Philistine and are you not servants of Saul? Choose a man for yourselves and have him come down to me.
9_17_8.MSG: Goliath stood there and called out to the Israelite troops, "Why bother using your whole army? Am I not Philistine enough for you? And you're all committed to Saul, aren't you? So pick your best fighter and pit him against me.
9_17_8.NIV: Goliath stood and shouted to the ranks of Israel, “Why do you come out and line up for battle? Am I not a Philistine, and are you not the servants of Saul? Choose a man and have him come down to me.
9_17_8.NRSVCE: He stood and shouted to the ranks of Israel, “Why have you come out to draw up for battle? Am I not a Philistine, and are you not servants of Saul? Choose a man for yourselves, and let him come down to me.

9_17_9.NKJV: If he is able to fight with me and kill me, then we will be your servants. But if I prevail against him and kill him, then you shall be our servants and serve us.”
9_17_9.KJV: If he be able to fight with me, and to kill me, then will we be your servants: but if I prevail against him, and kill him, then shall ye be our servants, and serve us.
9_17_9.ESV: If he is able to fight with me and kill me, then we will be your servants. But if I prevail against him and kill him, then you shall be our servants and serve us.”
9_17_9.NASB: 'If he is able to fight with me and kill me, then we will become your servants; but if I prevail against him and kill him, then you shall become our servants and serve us.'
9_17_9.NLT: If he kills me, then we will be your slaves. But if I kill him, you will be our slaves!
9_17_9.WEB: If he is able to fight with me and kill me, then will we be your servants; but if I prevail against him and kill him, then you will be our servants and serve us.”
9_17_9.CSB17: If he wins in a fight against me and kills me, we will be your servants. But if I win against him and kill him, then you will be our servants and serve us.”
9_17_9.AMP: If he is able to fight with me and kill me, then we will become your servants; but if I prevail against him and kill him, then you shall become our servants and serve us."
9_17_9.MSG: If he gets the upper hand and kills me, the Philistines will all become your slaves. But if I get the upper hand and kill him, you'll all become our slaves and serve us.
9_17_9.NIV: If he is able to fight and kill me, we will become your subjects; but if I overcome him and kill him, you will become our subjects and serve us.”
9_17_9.NRSVCE: If he is able to fight with me and kill me, then we will be your servants; but if I prevail against him and kill him, then you shall be our servants and serve us.”

9_17_10.NKJV: And the Philistine said, “I defy the armies of Israel this day; give me a man, that we may fight together.”
9_17_10.KJV: And the Philistine said, I defy the armies of Israel this day; give me a man, that we may fight together.
9_17_10.ESV: And the Philistine said, “I defy the ranks of Israel this day. Give me a man, that we may fight together.”
9_17_10.NASB: Again the Philistine said, 'I defy the ranks of Israel this day; give me a man that we may fight together.'
9_17_10.NLT: I defy the armies of Israel today! Send me a man who will fight me!”
9_17_10.WEB: The Philistine said, “I defy the armies of Israel today! Give me a man, that we may fight together!”
9_17_10.CSB17: Then the Philistine said, “I defy the ranks of Israel today. Send me a man so we can fight each other!”
9_17_10.AMP: Again the Philistine said, "I defy the battle lines of Israel this day; give me a man so that we may fight together."
9_17_10.MSG: I challenge the troops of Israel this day. Give me a man. Let us fight it out together!"
9_17_10.NIV: Then the Philistine said, “This day I defy the ranks of Israel! Give me a man and let us fight each other.”
9_17_10.NRSVCE: And the Philistine said, “Today I defy the ranks of Israel! Give me a man, that we may fight together.”

9_17_11.NKJV: When Saul and all Israel heard these words of the Philistine, they were dismayed and greatly afraid.
9_17_11.KJV: When Saul and all Israel heard those words of the Philistine, they were dismayed, and greatly afraid.
9_17_11.ESV: When Saul and all Israel heard these words of the Philistine, they were dismayed and greatly afraid.
9_17_11.NASB: When Saul and all Israel heard these words of the Philistine, they were dismayed and greatly afraid.
9_17_11.NLT: When Saul and the Israelites heard this, they were terrified and deeply shaken.
9_17_11.WEB: When Saul and all Israel heard those words of the Philistine, they were dismayed, and greatly afraid.
9_17_11.CSB17: When Saul and all Israel heard these words from the Philistine, they lost their courage and were terrified.
9_17_11.AMP: When Saul and all Israel heard these words of the Philistine, they were dismayed and greatly afraid.
9_17_11.MSG: When Saul and his troops heard the Philistine's challenge, they were terrified and lost all hope.
9_17_11.NIV: On hearing the Philistine’s words, Saul and all the Israelites were dismayed and terrified.
9_17_11.NRSVCE: When Saul and all Israel heard these words of the Philistine, they were dismayed and greatly afraid.

9_17_12.NKJV: Now David was the son of that Ephrathite of Bethlehem Judah, whose name was Jesse, and who had eight sons. And the man was old, advanced in years, in the days of Saul.
9_17_12.KJV: Now David was the son of that Ephrathite of Bethlehemjudah , whose name was Jesse; and he had eight sons: and the man went among men for an old man in the days of Saul.
9_17_12.ESV: Now David was the son of an Ephrathite of Bethlehem in Judah, named Jesse, who had eight sons. In the days of Saul the man was already old and advanced in years.
9_17_12.NASB: Now David was the son of the Ephrathite of Bethlehem in Judah, whose name was Jesse, and he had eight sons. And Jesse was old in the days of Saul, advanced [in years] among men.
9_17_12.NLT: Now David was the son of a man named Jesse, an Ephrathite from Bethlehem in the land of Judah. Jesse was an old man at that time, and he had eight sons.

9_17_12.MSG: Enter David. He was the son of Jesse the Ephrathite from Bethlehem in Judah. Jesse, the father of eight sons, was himself too old to join Saul's army.
9_17_12.NIV: Now David was the son of an Ephrathite named Jesse, who was from Bethlehem in Judah. Jesse had eight sons, and in Saul’s time he was old and well advanced in years.
9_17_12.NRSVCE: Now David was the son of an Ephrathite of Bethlehem in Judah, named Jesse, who had eight sons. In the days of Saul the man was already old and advanced in years.

9_17_13.MSG: Jesse's three older sons had followed Saul to war. The names of the three sons who had joined up with Saul were Eliab, the firstborn; next, Abinadab; and third, Shammah.

9_17_16.NRSVCE: For forty days the Philistine came forward and took his stand, morning and evening.

9_17_17.NKJV: Then Jesse said to his son David, “Take now for your brothers an ephah of this dried grain and these ten loaves, and run to your brothers at the camp.

9_17_17.NASB: Then Jesse said to David his son, 'Take now for your brothers an ephah of this roasted grain and these ten loaves and run to the camp to your brothers.
9_17_17.NLT: One day Jesse said to David, “Take this basket of roasted grain and these ten loaves of bread, and carry them quickly to your brothers.

9_17_17.AMP: Then Jesse said to David his son, "Take for your brothers an ephah of this roasted grain and these ten loaves of bread and run quickly to the camp to your brothers.

9_17_17.NIV: Now Jesse said to his son David, “Take this ephah of roasted grain and these ten loaves of bread for your brothers and hurry to their camp.

9_17_18.NKJV: And carry these ten cheeses to the captain of their thousand, and see how your brothers fare, and bring back news of them.”
9_17_18.KJV: And carry these ten cheeses unto the captain of their thousand, and look how thy brethren fare, and take their pledge. cheeses: Heb. cheeses of milk of: Heb. of a thousand
9_17_18.ESV: Also take these ten cheeses to the commander of their thousand. See if your brothers are well, and bring some token from them.”
9_17_18.NASB: 'Bring also these ten cuts of cheese to the commander of [their] thousand, and look into the welfare of your brothers, and bring back news of them.

9_17_25.NKJV: So the men of Israel said, “Have you seen this man who has come up? Surely he has come up to defy Israel; and it shall be that the man who kills him the king will enrich with great riches, will give him his daughter, and give his father’s house exemption from taxes in Israel.”
9_17_25.KJV: And the men of Israel said, Have ye seen this man that is come up? surely to defy Israel is he come up: and it shall be, that the man who killeth him, the king will enrich him with great riches, and will give him his daughter, and make his father's house free in Israel.
9_17_25.ESV: And the men of Israel said, “Have you seen this man who has come up? Surely he has come up to defy Israel. And the king will enrich the man who kills him with great riches and will give him his daughter and make his father's house free in Israel.”
9_17_25.NASB: The men of Israel said, 'Have you seen this man who is coming up? Surely he is coming up to defy Israel. And it will be that the king will enrich the man who kills him with great riches and will give him his daughter and make his father's house free in Israel.'
9_17_25.NLT: “Have you seen the giant?” the men asked. “He comes out each day to defy Israel. The king has offered a huge reward to anyone who kills him. He will give that man one of his daughters for a wife, and the man’s entire family will be exempted from paying taxes!”
9_17_25.WEB: The men of Israel said, “Have you seen this man who has come up? He has surely come up to defy Israel. The king will give great riches to the man who kills him, and will give him his daughter, and make his father’s house free in Israel.”

The king will make the man who kills him very rich and will give him his daughter. The king will also make the family of that man’s father exempt from paying taxes in Israel.”
9_17_25.AMP: The men of Israel said, "Have you seen this man who is coming up? Surely he is coming up to defy Israel. The king will reward the man who kills him with great riches, and will give him his daughter [in marriage] and make his father's house (family) free [from taxes and service] in Israel."
9_17_25.MSG: The talk among the troops was, "Have you ever seen anything like this, this man openly and defiantly challenging Israel? The man who kills the giant will have it made. The king will give him a huge reward, offer his daughter as a bride, and give his entire family a free ride."
9_17_25.NIV: Now the Israelites had been saying, “Do you see how this man keeps coming out? He comes out to defy Israel. The king will give great wealth to the man who kills him. He will also give him his daughter in marriage and will exempt his father’s family from taxes in Israel.”
9_17_25.NRSVCE: The Israelites said, “Have you seen this man who has come up? Surely he has come up to defy Israel. The king will greatly enrich the man who kills him, and will give him his daughter and make his family free in Israel.”

9_17_26.NKJV: Then David spoke to the men who stood by him, saying, “What shall be done for the man who kills this Philistine and takes away the reproach from Israel? For who is this uncircumcised Philistine, that he should defy the armies of the living God?”

9_17_26.ESV: And David said to the men who stood by him, “What shall be done for the man who kills this Philistine and takes away the reproach from Israel? For who is this uncircumcised Philistine, that he should defy the armies of the living God?”
9_17_26.NASB: Then David spoke to the men who were standing by him, saying, 'What will be done for the man who kills this Philistine and takes away the reproach from Israel? For who is this uncircumcised Philistine, that he should taunt the armies of the living God?'

9_17_26.MSG: David, who was talking to the men standing around him, asked, "What's in it for the man who kills that Philistine and gets rid of this ugly blot on Israel's honor? Who does he think he is, anyway, this uncircumcised Philistine, taunting the armies of God-Alive?"

9_17_28.ESV: Now Eliab his eldest brother heard when he spoke to the men. And Eliab's anger was kindled against David, and he said, “Why have you come down? And with whom have you left those few sheep in the wilderness? I know your presumption and the evil of your heart, for you have come down to see the battle.”
9_17_28.NASB: Now Eliab his oldest brother heard when he spoke to the men; and Eliab's anger burned against David and he said, 'Why have you come down? And with whom have you left those few sheep in the wilderness? I know your insolence and the wickedness of your heart; for you have come down in order to see the battle.'
9_17_28.NLT: But when David’s oldest brother, Eliab, heard David talking to the men, he was angry. “What are you doing around here anyway?” he demanded. “What about those few sheep you’re supposed to be taking care of? I know about your pride and deceit. You just want to see the battle!”
9_17_28.WEB: Eliab his oldest brother heard when he spoke to the men; and Eliab’s anger burned against David, and he said, “Why have you come down? With whom have you left those few sheep in the wilderness? I know your pride, and the naughtiness of your heart; for you have come down that you might see the battle.”
9_17_28.CSB17: David’s oldest brother Eliab listened as he spoke to the men, and he became angry with him. “Why did you come down here?” he asked. “Who did you leave those few sheep with in the wilderness? I know your arrogance and your evil heart — you came down to see the battle!”
9_17_28.AMP: Now Eliab his oldest brother heard what he said to the men; and Eliab's anger burned against David and he said, "Why have you come down here? With whom did you leave those few sheep in the wilderness? I know your presumption (overconfidence) and the evil of your heart; for you have come down in order to see the battle."
9_17_28.MSG: Eliab, his older brother, heard David fraternizing with the men and lost his temper: "What are you doing here! Why aren't you minding your own business, tending that scrawny flock of sheep? I know what you're up to. You've come down here to see the sights, hoping for a ringside seat at a bloody battle!"
9_17_28.NIV: When Eliab, David’s oldest brother, heard him speaking with the men, he burned with anger at him and asked, “Why have you come down here? And with whom did you leave those few sheep in the desert? I know how conceited you are and how wicked your heart is; you came down only to watch the battle.”

With whom have you left those few sheep in the wilderness? I know your presumption and the evil of your heart; for you have come down just to see the battle.”

9_17_48.NRSVCE: When the Philistine drew nearer to meet David, David ran quickly toward the battle line to meet the Philistine.

9_17_49.NKJV: Then David put his hand in his bag and took out a stone; and he slung it and struck the Philistine in his forehead, so that the stone sank into his forehead, and he fell on his face to the earth.

9_17_49.ESV: And David put his hand in his bag and took out a stone and slung it and struck the Philistine on his forehead. The stone sank into his forehead, and he fell on his face to the ground.
9_17_49.NASB: And David put his hand into his bag and took from it a stone and slung [it], and struck the Philistine on his forehead. And the stone sank into his forehead, so that he fell on his face to the ground.
9_17_49.NLT: Reaching into his shepherd’s bag and taking out a stone, he hurled it with his sling and hit the Philistine in the forehead. The stone sank in, and Goliath stumbled and fell face down on the ground.
9_17_49.WEB: David put his hand in his bag, took a stone, and slung it, and struck the Philistine in his forehead. The stone sank into his forehead, and he fell on his face to the earth.
9_17_49.CSB17: David put his hand in the bag, took out a stone, slung it, and hit the Philistine on his forehead. The stone sank into his forehead, and he fell facedown to the ground.
9_17_49.AMP: David put his hand into his bag and took out a stone and slung it, and it struck the Philistine on his forehead. The stone penetrated his forehead, and he fell face down on the ground.
9_17_49.MSG: David reached into his pocket for a stone, slung it, and hit the Philistine hard in the forehead, embedding the stone deeply. The Philistine crashed, facedown in the dirt.
9_17_49.NIV: Reaching into his bag and taking out a stone, he slung it and struck the Philistine on the forehead. The stone sank into his forehead, and he fell facedown on the ground.
9_17_49.NRSVCE: David put his hand in his bag, took out a stone, slung it, and struck the Philistine on his forehead; the stone sank into his forehead, and he fell face down on the ground.

9_17_50.NKJV: So David prevailed over the Philistine with a sling and a stone, and struck the Philistine and killed him. But there was no sword in the hand of David.
9_17_50.KJV: So David prevailed over the Philistine with a sling and with a stone, and smote the Philistine, and slew him; but there was no sword in the hand of David.
9_17_50.ESV: So David prevailed over the Philistine with a sling and with a stone, and struck the Philistine and killed him. There was no sword in the hand of David.
9_17_50.NASB: Thus David prevailed over the Philistine with a sling and a stone, and he struck the Philistine and killed him; but there was no sword in David's hand.
9_17_50.NLT: So David triumphed over the Philistine with only a sling and a stone, for he had no sword.
9_17_50.WEB: So David prevailed over the Philistine with a sling and with a stone, and struck the Philistine, and killed him; but there was no sword in the hand of David.
9_17_50.CSB17: David defeated the Philistine with a sling and a stone. David overpowered the Philistine and killed him without having a sword.
9_17_50.AMP: So David triumphed over the Philistine with a sling and a stone, and he struck down the Philistine and killed him; but there was no sword in David's hand.
9_17_50.MSG: That's how David beat the Philistine—with a sling and a stone. He hit him and killed him. No sword for David!
9_17_50.NIV: So David triumphed over the Philistine with a sling and a stone; without a sword in his hand he struck down the Philistine and killed him.
9_17_50.NRSVCE: So David prevailed over the Philistine with a sling and a stone, striking down the Philistine and killing him; there was no sword in David’s hand.

9_17_51.NKJV: Therefore David ran and stood over the Philistine, took his sword and drew it out of its sheath and killed him, and cut off his head with it. And when the Philistines saw that their champion was dead, they fled.
9_17_51.KJV: Therefore David ran, and stood upon the Philistine, and took his sword, and drew it out of the sheath thereof, and slew him, and cut off his head therewith. And when the Philistines saw their champion was dead, they fled.
9_17_51.ESV: Then David ran and stood over the Philistine and took his sword and drew it out of its sheath and killed him and cut off his head with it. When the Philistines saw that their champion was dead, they fled.
9_17_51.NASB: Then David ran and stood over the Philistine and took his sword and drew it out of its sheath and killed him, and cut off his head with it. When the Philistines saw that their champion was dead, they fled.
9_17_51.NLT: Then David ran over and pulled Goliath’s sword from its sheath. David used it to kill him and cut off his head. Israel Routs the Philistines When the Philistines saw that their champion was dead, they turned and ran.
9_17_51.WEB: Then David ran, stood over the Philistine, took his sword, drew it out of its sheath, killed him, and cut off his head with it. When the Philistines saw that their champion was dead, they fled.
9_17_51.CSB17: David ran and stood over him. He grabbed the Philistine’s sword, pulled it from its sheath, and used it to kill him. Then he cut off his head. When the Philistines saw that their hero was dead, they fled.
9_17_51.AMP: So he ran and stood over the Philistine, grasped his sword and drew it out of its sheath and killed him, and cut off his head with it. When the Philistines saw that their [mighty] champion was dead, they fled.
9_17_51.MSG: Then David ran up to the Philistine and stood over him, pulled the giant's sword from its sheath, and finished the job by cutting off his head. When the Philistines saw that their great champion was dead, they scattered, running for their lives.
9_17_51.NIV: David ran and stood over him. He took hold of the Philistine’s sword and drew it from the scabbard. After he killed him, he cut off his head with the sword.When the Philistines saw that their hero was dead, they turned and ran.
9_17_51.NRSVCE: Then David ran and stood over the Philistine; he grasped his sword, drew it out of its sheath, and killed him; then he cut off his head with it. When the Philistines saw that their champion was dead, they fled.

9_17_52.NKJV: Now the men of Israel and Judah arose and shouted, and pursued the Philistines as far as the entrance of the valley and to the gates of Ekron. And the wounded of the Philistines fell along the road to Shaaraim, even as far as Gath and Ekron.
9_17_52.KJV: And the men of Israel and of Judah arose, and shouted, and pursued the Philistines, until thou come to the valley, and to the gates of Ekron. And the wounded of the Philistines fell down by the way to Shaaraim, even unto Gath, and unto Ekron.
9_17_52.ESV: And the men of Israel and Judah rose with a shout and pursued the Philistines as far as Gath and the gates of Ekron, so that the wounded Philistines fell on the way from Shaaraim as far as Gath and Ekron.
9_17_52.NASB: The men of Israel and Judah arose and shouted and pursued the Philistines as far as the valley, and to the gates of Ekron. And the slain Philistines lay along the way to Shaaraim, even to Gath and Ekron.
9_17_52.NLT: Then the men of Israel and Judah gave a great shout of triumph and rushed after the Philistines, chasing them as far as Gath and the gates of Ekron. The bodies of the dead and wounded Philistines were strewn all along the road from Shaaraim, as far as Gath and Ekron.
9_17_52.WEB: The men of Israel and of Judah arose and shouted, and pursued the Philistines as far as Gai and to the gates of Ekron. The wounded of the Philistines fell down by the way to Shaaraim, even to Gath and to Ekron.
9_17_52.CSB17: The men of Israel and Judah rallied, shouting their battle cry, and chased the Philistines to the entrance of the valley and to the gates of Ekron. Philistine bodies were strewn all along the Shaaraim road to Gath and Ekron.
9_17_52.AMP: The men of Israel and Judah stood with a shout and pursued the Philistines as far as the entrance to the valley and the gates of Ekron. And the [fatally] wounded Philistines fell along the way to Shaaraim, even as far as Gath and Ekron.
9_17_52.MSG: The men of Israel and Judah were up on their feet, shouting! They chased the Philistines all the way to the outskirts of Gath and the gates of Ekron.
9_17_52.NIV: Then the men of Israel and Judah surged forward with a shout and pursued the Philistines to the entrance of Gath and to the gates of Ekron. Their dead were strewn along the Shaaraim road to Gath and Ekron.
9_17_52.NRSVCE: The troops of Israel and Judah rose up with a shout and pursued the Philistines as far as Gath and the gates of Ekron, so that the wounded Philistines fell on the way from Shaaraim as far as Gath and Ekron.

9_17_53.NKJV: Then the children of Israel returned from chasing the Philistines, and they plundered their tents.
9_17_53.KJV: And the children of Israel returned from chasing after the Philistines, and they spoiled their tents.
9_17_53.ESV: And the people of Israel came back from chasing the Philistines, and they plundered their camp.
9_17_53.NASB: The sons of Israel returned from chasing the Philistines and plundered their camps.
9_17_53.NLT: Then the Israelite army returned and plundered the deserted Philistine camp.
9_17_53.WEB: The children of Israel returned from chasing after the Philistines and they plundered their camp.
9_17_53.CSB17: When the Israelites returned from the pursuit of the Philistines, they plundered their camps.
9_17_53.AMP: The sons of Israel returned from their pursuit of the Philistines and plundered their camp.
9_17_53.MSG: Wounded Philistines were strewn along the Shaaraim road all the way to Gath and Ekron. After chasing the Philistines, the Israelites came back and looted their camp.

9_17_53.NRSVCE: The Israelites came back from chasing the Philistines, and they plundered their camp.

9_17_54.NKJV: And David took the head of the Philistine and brought it to Jerusalem, but he put his armor in his tent.

9_17_54.NASB: Then David took the Philistine's head and brought it to Jerusalem, but he put his weapons in his tent.
9_17_54.NLT: (David took the Philistine’s head to Jerusalem, but he stored the man’s armor in his own tent.)

9_17_54.CSB17: David took Goliath’s head and brought it to Jerusalem, but he put Goliath’s weapons in his own tent.
9_17_54.AMP: Then David took the head of the Philistine and brought it to Jerusalem, but he put his weapons in his tent.
9_17_54.MSG: David took the Philistine's head and brought it to Jerusalem. But the giant's weapons he placed in his own tent.

9_17_54.NRSVCE: David took the head of the Philistine and brought it to Jerusalem; but he put his armor in his tent.

9_17_55.NKJV: When Saul saw David going out against the Philistine, he said to Abner, the commander of the army, “Abner, whose son is this youth?” And Abner said, “As your soul lives, O king, I do not know.”
9_17_55.KJV: And when Saul saw David go forth against the Philistine, he said unto Abner, the captain of the host, Abner, whose son is this youth? And Abner said, As thy soul liveth, O king, I cannot tell.
9_17_55.ESV: As soon as Saul saw David go out against the Philistine, he said to Abner, the commander of the army, “Abner, whose son is this youth?” And Abner said, “As your soul lives, O king, I do not know.”
9_17_55.NASB: Now when Saul saw David going out against the Philistine, he said to Abner the commander of the army, 'Abner, whose son is this young man?' And Abner said, 'By your life, O king, I do not know.'
9_17_55.NLT: As Saul watched David go out to fight the Philistine, he asked Abner, the commander of his army, “Abner, whose son is this young man?” “I really don’t know,” Abner declared.
9_17_55.WEB: When Saul saw David go out against the Philistine, he said to Abner, the captain of the army, “Abner, whose son is this youth?” Abner said, “As your soul lives, O king, I can’t tell.”
9_17_55.CSB17: When Saul had seen David going out to confront the Philistine, he asked Abner the commander of the army, “Whose son is this youth, Abner?” “Your Majesty, as surely as you live, I don’t know,” Abner replied.
9_17_55.AMP: When Saul saw David going out against the Philistine, he said to Abner the captain of the army, "Abner, whose son is this young man?" And Abner answered, "By your life, O king, I do not know."
9_17_55.MSG: When Saul saw David go out to meet the Philistine, he said to Abner, commander of the army, "Tell me about this young man's family."Abner said, "For the life of me, O King, I don't know."
9_17_55.NIV: As Saul watched David going out to meet the Philistine, he said to Abner, commander of the army, “Abner, whose son is that young man?”Abner replied, “As surely as you live, O king, I don’t know.”
9_17_55.NRSVCE: When Saul saw David go out against the Philistine, he said to Abner, the commander of the army, “Abner, whose son is this young man?” Abner said, “As your soul lives, O king, I do not know.”

9_17_56.NIV: The king said, “Find out whose son this young man is.”
9_17_56.NRSVCE: The king said, “Inquire whose son the stripling is.”

9_17_57.NKJV: Then, as David returned from the slaughter of the Philistine, Abner took him and brought him before Saul with the head of the Philistine in his hand.
9_17_57.KJV: And as David returned from the slaughter of the Philistine, Abner took him, and brought him before Saul with the head of the Philistine in his hand.

9_17_57.NRSVCE: On David’s return from killing the Philistine, Abner took him and brought him before Saul, with the head of the Philistine in his hand.

9_17_58.NKJV: And Saul said to him, “Whose son are you, young man?” So David answered, “I am the son of your servant Jesse the Bethlehemite.”
9_17_58.KJV: And Saul said to him, Whose son art thou, thou young man? And David answered, I am the son of thy servant Jesse the Bethlehemite.
9_17_58.ESV: And Saul said to him, “Whose son are you, young man?” And David answered, “I am the son of your servant Jesse the Bethlehemite.”
9_17_58.NASB: Saul said to him, 'Whose son are you, young man?' And David answered, '[I am] the son of your servant Jesse the Bethlehemite.'
9_17_58.NLT: “Tell me about your father, young man,” Saul said. And David replied, “His name is Jesse, and we live in Bethlehem.”
9_17_58.WEB: Saul said to him, “Whose son are you, you young man?” David answered, “I am the son of your servant Jesse the Bethlehemite.”
9_17_58.CSB17: Saul said to him, “Whose son are you, young man?” “The son of your servant Jesse of Bethlehem,” David answered.
9_17_58.AMP: Saul asked him, "Whose son are you, young man?" And David answered, "I am the son of your servant Jesse of Bethlehem."
9_17_58.MSG: Saul asked him, "Young man, whose son are you?""I'm the son of your servant Jesse," said David, "the one who lives in Bethlehem."
9_17_58.NIV: “Whose son are you, young man?” Saul asked him.David said, “I am the son of your servant Jesse of Bethlehem.”
9_17_58.NRSVCE: Saul said to him, “Whose son are you, young man?” And David answered, “I am the son of your servant Jesse the Bethlehemite.”

9_18_1.NKJV: Now when he had finished speaking to Saul, the soul of Jonathan was knit to the soul of David, and Jonathan loved him as his own soul.
9_18_1.KJV: And it came to pass, when he had made an end of speaking unto Saul, that the soul of Jonathan was knit with the soul of David, and Jonathan loved him as his own soul.
9_18_1.ESV: As soon as he had finished speaking to Saul, the soul of Jonathan was knit to the soul of David, and Jonathan loved him as his own soul.
9_18_1.NASB: Now it came about when he had finished speaking to Saul, that the soul of Jonathan was knit to the soul of David, and Jonathan loved him as himself.
9_18_1.NLT: After David had finished talking with Saul, he met Jonathan, the king’s son. There was an immediate bond between them, for Jonathan loved David.
9_18_1.WEB: When he had finished speaking to Saul, the soul of Jonathan was knit with the soul of David, and Jonathan loved him as his own soul.
9_18_1.CSB17: When David had finished speaking with Saul, Jonathan was bound to David in close friendship, and loved him as much as he loved himself.
9_18_1.AMP: When David had finished speaking to Saul, the soul of Jonathan was bonded to the soul of David, and Jonathan loved him as himself.
9_18_1.MSG: By the time David had finished reporting to Saul, Jonathan was deeply impressed with David—an immediate bond was forged between them. He became totally committed to David. From that point on he would be David's number-one advocate and friend.
9_18_1.NIV: Saul’s Jealousy of DavidAfter David had finished talking with Saul, Jonathan became one in spirit with David, and he loved him as himself.
9_18_1.NRSVCE: When David had finished speaking to Saul, the soul of Jonathan was bound to the soul of David, and Jonathan loved him as his own soul.

9_18_2.NKJV: Saul took him that day, and would not let him go home to his father’s house anymore.
9_18_2.KJV: And Saul took him that day, and would let him go no more home to his father's house.
9_18_2.ESV: And Saul took him that day and would not let him return to his father's house.
9_18_2.NASB: Saul took him that day and did not let him return to his father's house.
9_18_2.NLT: From that day on Saul kept David with him and wouldn’t let him return home.
9_18_2.WEB: Saul took him that day, and would let him go no more home to his father’s house.
9_18_2.CSB17: Saul kept David with him from that day on and did not let him return to his father’s house.
9_18_2.AMP: Saul took David that day and did not let him return to his father's house.
9_18_2.MSG: Saul received David into his own household that day, no more to return to the home of his father.
9_18_2.NIV: From that day Saul kept David with him and did not let him return to his father’s house.
9_18_2.NRSVCE: Saul took him that day and would not let him return to his father’s house.

9_18_3.NKJV: Then Jonathan and David made a covenant, because he loved him as his own soul.
9_18_3.KJV: Then Jonathan and David made a covenant, because he loved him as his own soul.
9_18_3.ESV: Then Jonathan made a covenant with David, because he loved him as his own soul.
9_18_3.NASB: Then Jonathan made a covenant with David because he loved him as himself.
9_18_3.NLT: And Jonathan made a solemn pact with David, because he loved him as he loved himself.
9_18_3.WEB: Then Jonathan and David made a covenant, because he loved him as his own soul.
9_18_3.CSB17: Jonathan made a covenant with David because he loved him as much as himself.
9_18_3.AMP: Then Jonathan made a covenant with David because he loved him as himself.
9_18_3.MSG: Jonathan, out of his deep love for David, made a covenant with him.
9_18_3.NIV: And Jonathan made a covenant with David because he loved him as himself.
9_18_3.NRSVCE: Then Jonathan made a covenant with David, because he loved him as his own soul.

9_18_4.NKJV: And Jonathan took off the robe that was on him and gave it to David, with his armor, even to his sword and his bow and his belt.
9_18_4.KJV: And Jonathan stripped himself of the robe that was upon him, and gave it to David, and his garments, even to his sword, and to his bow, and to his girdle.
9_18_4.ESV: And Jonathan stripped himself of the robe that was on him and gave it to David, and his armor, and even his sword and his bow and his belt.
9_18_4.NASB: Jonathan stripped himself of the robe that was on him and gave it to David, with his armor, including his sword and his bow and his belt.
9_18_4.NLT: Jonathan sealed the pact by taking off his robe and giving it to David, together with his tunic, sword, bow, and belt.
9_18_4.WEB: Jonathan stripped himself of the robe that was on him, and gave it to David, and his clothing, even including his sword, his bow, and his sash.
9_18_4.CSB17: Then Jonathan removed the robe he was wearing and gave it to David, along with his military tunic, his sword, his bow, and his belt.
9_18_4.AMP: Jonathan stripped himself of the outer robe that he was wearing and gave it to David, with his armor, including his sword, his bow, and his belt.
9_18_4.MSG: He formalized it with solemn gifts: his own royal robe and weapons—armor, sword, bow, and belt.
9_18_4.NIV: Jonathan took off the robe he was wearing and gave it to David, along with his tunic, and even his sword, his bow and his belt.
9_18_4.NRSVCE: Jonathan stripped himself of the robe that he was wearing, and gave it to David, and his armor, and even his sword and his bow and his belt.

9_18_5.NKJV: So David went out wherever Saul sent him, and behaved wisely. And Saul set him over the men of war, and he was accepted in the sight of all the people and also in the sight of Saul’s servants.
9_18_5.KJV: And David went out whithersoever Saul sent him, and behaved himself wisely: and Saul set him over the men of war, and he was accepted in the sight of all the people, and also in the sight of Saul's servants. behaved: or, prospered
9_18_5.ESV: And David went out and was successful wherever Saul sent him, so that Saul set him over the men of war. And this was good in the sight of all the people and also in the sight of Saul's servants.
9_18_5.NASB: So David went out wherever Saul sent him, [and] prospered; and Saul set him over the men of war. And it was pleasing in the sight of all the people and also in the sight of Saul's servants.
9_18_5.NLT: Whatever Saul asked David to do, David did it successfully. So Saul made him a commander over the men of war, an appointment that was welcomed by the people and Saul’s officers alike.
9_18_5.WEB: David went out wherever Saul sent him, and behaved himself wisely; and Saul set him over the men of war. It was good in the sight of all the people, and also in the sight of Saul’s servants.
9_18_5.CSB17: David marched out with the army and was successful in everything Saul sent him to do. Saul put him in command of the fighting men, which pleased all the people and Saul’s servants as well.
9_18_5.AMP: So David went out wherever Saul sent him, and he acted wisely and prospered; and Saul appointed him over the men of war. And it pleased all the people and also Saul's servants.
9_18_5.MSG: Whatever Saul gave David to do, he did it—and did it well. So well that Saul put him in charge of his military operations. Everybody, both the people in general and Saul's servants, approved of and admired David's leadership.
9_18_5.NIV: Whatever Saul sent him to do, David did it so successfully that Saul gave him a high rank in the army. This pleased all the people, and Saul’s officers as well.
9_18_5.NRSVCE: David went out and was successful wherever Saul sent him; as a result, Saul set him over the army. And all the people, even the servants of Saul, approved.

9_18_6.NKJV: Now it had happened as they were coming home, when David was returning from the slaughter of the Philistine, that the women had come out of all the cities of Israel, singing and dancing, to meet King Saul, with tambourines, with joy, and with musical instruments.
9_18_6.KJV: And it came to pass as they came, when David was returned from the slaughter of the Philistine, that the women came out of all cities of Israel, singing and dancing, to meet king Saul, with tabrets, with joy, and with instruments of musick. Philistine: or, Philistines instruments: Heb. three stringed instruments
9_18_6.ESV: As they were coming home, when David returned from striking down the Philistine, the women came out of all the cities of Israel, singing and dancing, to meet King Saul, with tambourines, with songs of joy, and with musical instruments.
9_18_6.NASB: It happened as they were coming, when David returned from killing the Philistine, that the women came out of all the cities of Israel, singing and dancing, to meet King Saul, with tambourines, with joy and with musical instruments.
9_18_6.NLT: When the victorious Israelite army was returning home after David had killed the Philistine, women from all the towns of Israel came out to meet King Saul. They sang and danced for joy with tambourines and cymbals.
9_18_6.WEB: As they came, when David returned from the slaughter of the Philistine, the women came out of all the cities of Israel, singing and dancing, to meet king Saul, with tambourines, with joy, and with instruments of music.
9_18_6.CSB17: As the troops were coming back, when David was returning from killing the Philistine, the women came out from all the cities of Israel to meet King Saul, singing and dancing with tambourines, with shouts of joy, and with three-stringed instruments.
9_18_6.AMP: As they were coming [home], when David returned from killing the Philistine, the women came out of all the cities of Israel, singing and dancing, to meet King Saul with tambourines, [songs of] joy, and musical instruments.
9_18_6.MSG: As they returned home, after David had killed the Philistine, the women poured out of all the villages of Israel singing and dancing, welcoming King Saul with tambourines, festive songs, and lutes.
9_18_6.NIV: When the men were returning home after David had killed the Philistine, the women came out from all the towns of Israel to meet King Saul with singing and dancing, with joyful songs and with tambourines and lutes.
9_18_6.NRSVCE: As they were coming home, when David returned from killing the Philistine, the women came out of all the towns of Israel, singing and dancing, to meet King Saul, with tambourines, with songs of joy, and with musical instruments.

9_18_7.NKJV: So the women sang as they danced, and said: “Saul has slain his thousands, And David his ten thousands.”
9_18_7.KJV: And the women answered one another as they played, and said, Saul hath slain his thousands, and David his ten thousands.
9_18_7.ESV: And the women sang to one another as they celebrated, “Saul has struck down his thousands, and David his ten thousands.”
9_18_7.NASB: The women sang as they played, and said, 'Saul has slain his thousands, And David his ten thousands.'
9_18_7.NLT: This was their song: “Saul has killed his thousands,and David his ten thousands!”
9_18_7.WEB: The women sang to one another as they played, and said, “Saul has slain his thousands, and David his ten thousands.”
9_18_7.CSB17: As they danced, the women sang: Saul has killed his thousands, but David his tens of thousands.
9_18_7.AMP: The women sang as they played and danced, saying, "Saul has slain his thousands, And David his ten thousands."
9_18_7.MSG: In playful frolic the women sang,Saul kills by the thousand,David by the ten thousand!
9_18_7.NIV: As they danced, they sang:“Saul has slain his thousands,and David his tens of thousands.”
9_18_7.NRSVCE: And the women sang to one another as they made merry, “Saul has killed his thousands, and David his ten thousands.”

9_18_8.NKJV: Then Saul was very angry, and the saying displeased him; and he said, “They have ascribed to David ten thousands, and to me they have ascribed only thousands. Now what more can he have but the kingdom?”
9_18_8.KJV: And Saul was very wroth, and the saying displeased him; and he said, They have ascribed unto David ten thousands, and to me they have ascribed but thousands: and what can he have more but the kingdom? displeased him: Heb. was evil in his eyes
9_18_8.ESV: And Saul was very angry, and this saying displeased him. He said, “They have ascribed to David ten thousands, and to me they have ascribed thousands, and what more can he have but the kingdom?”
9_18_8.NASB: Then Saul became very angry, for this saying displeased him; and he said, 'They have ascribed to David ten thousands, but to me they have ascribed thousands. Now what more can he have but the kingdom?'
9_18_8.NLT: This made Saul very angry. “What’s this?” he said. “They credit David with ten thousands and me with only thousands. Next they’ll be making him their king!”
9_18_8.WEB: Saul was very angry, and this saying displeased him. He said, “They have ascribed to David ten thousands, and to me they have ascribed only thousands. What can he have more but the kingdom?”
9_18_8.CSB17: Saul was furious and resented this song. “They credited tens of thousands to David,” he complained, “but they only credited me with thousands. What more can he have but the kingdom?”
9_18_8.AMP: Then Saul became very angry, for this saying displeased him; and he said, "They have ascribed to David ten thousands, but to me they have ascribed [only] thousands. Now what more can he have but the kingdom?"
9_18_8.MSG: This made Saul angry—very angry. He took it as a personal insult. He said, "They credit David with 'ten thousands' and me with only 'thousands.' Before you know it they'll be giving him the kingdom!"
9_18_8.NIV: Saul was very angry; this refrain galled him. “They have credited David with tens of thousands,” he thought, “but me with only thousands. What more can he get but the kingdom?”
9_18_8.NRSVCE: Saul was very angry, for this saying displeased him. He said, “They have ascribed to David ten thousands, and to me they have ascribed thousands; what more can he have but the kingdom?”

9_18_9.NKJV: So Saul eyed David from that day forward.
9_18_9.KJV: And Saul eyed David from that day and forward.
9_18_9.ESV: And Saul eyed David from that day on.
9_18_9.NASB: Saul looked at David with suspicion from that day on.
9_18_9.NLT: So from that time on Saul kept a jealous eye on David.
9_18_9.WEB: Saul watched David from that day and forward.
9_18_9.CSB17: So Saul watched David jealously from that day forward.
9_18_9.AMP: Saul looked at David with suspicion [and jealously] from that day forward.
9_18_9.MSG: From that moment on, Saul kept his eye on David.
9_18_9.NIV: And from that time on Saul kept a jealous eye on David.
9_18_9.NRSVCE: So Saul eyed David from that day on.

9_18_10.NKJV: And it happened on the next day that the distressing spirit from God came upon Saul, and he prophesied inside the house. So David played music with his hand, as at other times; but there was a spear in Saul’s hand.
9_18_10.KJV: And it came to pass on the morrow, that the evil spirit from God came upon Saul, and he prophesied in the midst of the house: and David played with his hand, as at other times: and there was a javelin in Saul's hand.
9_18_10.ESV: The next day a harmful spirit from God rushed upon Saul, and he raved within his house while David was playing the lyre, as he did day by day. Saul had his spear in his hand.
9_18_10.NASB: Now it came about on the next day that an evil spirit from God came mightily upon Saul, and he raved in the midst of the house, while David was playing [the harp] with his hand, as usual; and a spear [was] in Saul's hand.
9_18_10.NLT: The very next day a tormenting spirit from God overwhelmed Saul, and he began to rave in his house like a madman. David was playing the harp, as he did each day. But Saul had a spear in his hand,
9_18_10.WEB: On the next day, an evil spirit from God came mightily on Saul, and he prophesied in the middle of the house. David played with his hand, as he did day by day. Saul had his spear in his hand;
9_18_10.CSB17: The next day an evil spirit sent from God came powerfully on Saul, and he began to rave inside the palace. David was playing the lyre as usual, but Saul was holding a spear,
9_18_10.AMP: Now it came about on the next day that an evil spirit from God came forcefully on Saul, and he raved [madly] inside his house, while David was playing the harp with his hand, as usual; and there was a spear in Saul's hand.
9_18_10.MSG: The next day an ugly mood was sent by God to afflict Saul, who became quite beside himself, raving. David played his harp, as he usually did at such times. Saul had a spear in his hand.
9_18_10.NIV: The next day an evil spirit from God came forcefully upon Saul. He was prophesying in his house, while David was playing the harp, as he usually did. Saul had a spear in his hand
9_18_10.NRSVCE: The next day an evil spirit from God rushed upon Saul, and he raved within his house, while David was playing the lyre, as he did day by day. Saul had his spear in his hand;

9_18_11.NKJV: And Saul cast the spear, for he said, “I will pin David to the wall!” But David escaped his presence twice.
9_18_11.KJV: And Saul cast the javelin; for he said, I will smite David even to the wall with it. And David avoided out of his presence twice.
9_18_11.ESV: And Saul hurled the spear, for he thought, “I will pin David to the wall.” But David evaded him twice.
9_18_11.NASB: Saul hurled the spear for he thought, 'I will pin David to the wall.' But David escaped from his presence twice.
9_18_11.NLT: and he suddenly hurled it at David, intending to pin him to the wall. But David escaped him twice.
9_18_11.WEB: and Saul threw the spear, for he said, “I will pin David even to the wall!” David escaped from his presence twice.
9_18_11.CSB17: and he threw it, thinking, “I’ll pin David to the wall.” But David got away from him twice.
9_18_11.AMP: Saul hurled the spear, for he thought, "I will pin David to the wall." But David evaded him twice.
9_18_11.MSG: Suddenly Saul threw the spear, thinking, "I'll nail David to the wall." David ducked, and the spear missed. This happened twice.
9_18_11.NIV: and he hurled it, saying to himself, “I’ll pin David to the wall.” But David eluded him twice.
9_18_11.NRSVCE: and Saul threw the spear, for he thought, “I will pin David to the wall.” But David eluded him twice.

9_18_12.NKJV: Now Saul was afraid of David, because the LORD was with him, but had departed from Saul.
9_18_12.KJV: And Saul was afraid of David, because the LORD was with him, and was departed from Saul.
9_18_12.ESV: Saul was afraid of David because the LORD was with him but had departed from Saul.
9_18_12.NASB: Now Saul was afraid of David, for the LORD was with him but had departed from Saul.
9_18_12.NLT: Saul was then afraid of David, for the LORD was with David and had turned away from Saul.
9_18_12.WEB: Saul was afraid of David, because Yahweh was with him, and had departed from Saul.
9_18_12.CSB17: Saul was afraid of David, because the LORD was with David but had left Saul.
9_18_12.AMP: Now Saul was afraid of David, because the LORD was with him, but had departed from Saul.
9_18_12.MSG: Now Saul feared David. It was clear that God was with David and had left Saul.
9_18_12.NIV: Saul was afraid of David, because the Lord was with David but had left Saul.
9_18_12.NRSVCE: Saul was afraid of David, because the LORD was with him but had departed from Saul.

9_18_13.NKJV: Therefore Saul removed him from his presence, and made him his captain over a thousand; and he went out and came in before the people.
9_18_13.KJV: Therefore Saul removed him from him, and made him his captain over a thousand; and he went out and came in before the people.
9_18_13.ESV: So Saul removed him from his presence and made him a commander of a thousand. And he went out and came in before the people.
9_18_13.NASB: Therefore Saul removed him from his presence and appointed him as his commander of a thousand; and he went out and came in before the people.
9_18_13.NLT: Finally, Saul sent him away and appointed him commander over 1,000 men, and David faithfully led his troops into battle.
9_18_13.WEB: Therefore Saul removed him from his presence, and made him his captain over a thousand; and he went out and came in before the people.
9_18_13.CSB17: Therefore, Saul sent David away from him and made him commander over a thousand men. David led the troops
9_18_13.AMP: So Saul had David removed from his presence and appointed him as his commander of a thousand; and he publicly associated with the people.
9_18_13.MSG: So, Saul got David out of his sight by making him an officer in the army. David was in combat frequently.
9_18_13.NIV: So he sent David away from him and gave him command over a thousand men, and David led the troops in their campaigns.
9_18_13.NRSVCE: So Saul removed him from his presence, and made him a commander of a thousand; and David marched out and came in, leading the army.

9_18_14.NKJV: And David behaved wisely in all his ways, and the LORD was with him.
9_18_14.KJV: And David behaved himself wisely in all his ways; and the LORD was with him. behaved: or, prospered
9_18_14.ESV: And David had success in all his undertakings, for the LORD was with him.
9_18_14.NASB: David was prospering in all his ways for the LORD [was] with him.
9_18_14.NLT: David continued to succeed in everything he did, for the LORD was with him.
9_18_14.WEB: David behaved himself wisely in all his ways; and Yahweh was with him.
9_18_14.CSB17: and continued to be successful in all his activities because the LORD was with him.
9_18_14.AMP: David acted wisely and prospered in all his ways, and the LORD was with him.
9_18_14.MSG: Everything David did turned out well. Yes, God was with him.
9_18_14.NIV: In everything he did he had great success, because the Lord was with him.
9_18_14.NRSVCE: David had success in all his undertakings; for the LORD was with him.

9_18_15.NKJV: Therefore, when Saul saw that he behaved very wisely, he was afraid of him.
9_18_15.KJV: Wherefore when Saul saw that he behaved himself very wisely, he was afraid of him.
9_18_15.ESV: And when Saul saw that he had great success, he stood in fearful awe of him.
9_18_15.NASB: When Saul saw that he was prospering greatly, he dreaded him.
9_18_15.NLT: When Saul recognized this, he became even more afraid of him.
9_18_15.WEB: When Saul saw that he behaved himself very wisely, he stood in awe of him.
9_18_15.CSB17: When Saul observed that David was very successful, he dreaded him.
9_18_15.AMP: When Saul saw that he was prospering greatly, he was afraid of him.
9_18_15.MSG: As Saul saw David becoming more successful, he himself grew more fearful. He could see the handwriting on the wall.
9_18_15.NIV: When Saul saw how successful he was, he was afraid of him.
9_18_15.NRSVCE: When Saul saw that he had great success, he stood in awe of him.

9_18_16.NKJV: But all Israel and Judah loved David, because he went out and came in before them.
9_18_16.KJV: But all Israel and Judah loved David, because he went out and came in before them.
9_18_16.ESV: But all Israel and Judah loved David, for he went out and came in before them.
9_18_16.NASB: But all Israel and Judah loved David, and he went out and came in before them.
9_18_16.NLT: But all Israel and Judah loved David because he was so successful at leading his troops into battle.
9_18_16.WEB: But all Israel and Judah loved David; for he went out and came in before them.
9_18_16.CSB17: But all Israel and Judah loved David because he was leading their troops.
9_18_16.AMP: But all Israel and Judah loved David, because he publicly associated with them.
9_18_16.MSG: But everyone else in Israel and Judah loved David. They loved watching him in action.
9_18_16.NIV: But all Israel and Judah loved David, because he led them in their campaigns.
9_18_16.NRSVCE: But all Israel and Judah loved David; for it was he who marched out and came in leading them.

9_18_17.NKJV: Then Saul said to David, “Here is my older daughter Merab; I will give her to you as a wife. Only be valiant for me, and fight the LORD’s battles.” For Saul thought, “Let my hand not be against him, but let the hand of the Philistines be against him.”
9_18_17.KJV: And Saul said to David, Behold my elder daughter Merab, her will I give thee to wife: only be thou valiant for me, and fight the LORD'S battles. For Saul said, Let not mine hand be upon him, but let the hand of the Philistines be upon him. valiant: Heb. a son of valour
9_18_17.ESV: Then Saul said to David, “Here is my elder daughter Merab. I will give her to you for a wife. Only be valiant for me and fight the LORD's battles.” For Saul thought, “Let not my hand be against him, but let the hand of the Philistines be against him.”
9_18_17.NASB: Then Saul said to David, 'Here is my older daughter Merab; I will give her to you as a wife, only be a valiant man for me and fight the LORD'S battles.' For Saul thought, 'My hand shall not be against him, but let the hand of the Philistines be against him.'
9_18_17.NLT: One day Saul said to David, “I am ready to give you my older daughter, Merab, as your wife. But first you must prove yourself to be a real warrior by fighting the LORD’s battles.” For Saul thought, “I’ll send him out against the Philistines and let them kill him rather than doing it myself.”
9_18_17.WEB: Saul said to David, “Behold, my elder daughter Merab, I will give her to you as wife. Only be valiant for me, and fight Yahweh’s battles.” For Saul said, “Don’t let my hand be on him, but let the hand of the Philistines be on him.”
9_18_17.CSB17: Saul told David, “Here is my oldest daughter Merab. I’ll give her to you as a wife, if you will be a warrior for me and fight the LORD’s battles.” But Saul was thinking, “I don’t need to raise a hand against him; let the hand of the Philistines be against him.”
9_18_17.AMP: Then Saul said to David, "Behold I will give you my older daughter Merab as a wife; only be brave for me and fight the Lord's battles." For Saul thought, "My hand shall not be against him, but let the hand of the Philistines be against him."
9_18_17.MSG: One day Saul said to David, "Here is Merab, my eldest daughter. I want to give her to you as your wife. Be brave and bold for my sake. Fight God's battles!" But all the time Saul was thinking, "The Philistines will kill him for me. I won't have to lift a hand against him."
9_18_17.NIV: Saul said to David, “Here is my older daughter Merab. I will give her to you in marriage; only serve me bravely and fight the battles of the Lord.” For Saul said to himself, “I will not raise a hand against him. Let the Philistines do that!”
9_18_17.NRSVCE: Then Saul said to David, “Here is my elder daughter Merab; I will give her to you as a wife; only be valiant for me and fight the LORD’s battles.” For Saul thought, “I will not raise a hand against him; let the Philistines deal with him.”

9_18_18.NKJV: So David said to Saul, “Who am I, and what is my life or my father’s family in Israel, that I should be son-in-law to the king?”
9_18_18.KJV: And David said unto Saul, Who am I? and what is my life, or my father's family in Israel, that I should be son in law to the king?
9_18_18.ESV: And David said to Saul, “Who am I, and who are my relatives, my father's clan in Israel, that I should be son-in-law to the king?”
9_18_18.NASB: But David said to Saul, 'Who am I, and what is my life [or] my father's family in Israel, that I should be the king's son-in-law?'
9_18_18.NLT: “Who am I, and what is my family in Israel that I should be the king’s son-in-law?” David exclaimed. “My father’s family is nothing!”
9_18_18.WEB: David said to Saul, “Who am I, and what is my life, or my father’s family in Israel, that I should be son-in-law to the king?”
9_18_18.CSB17: Then David responded, “Who am I, and what is my family or my father’s clan in Israel that I should become the king’s son-in-law?”
9_18_18.AMP: David said to Saul, "Who am I, and what is my life or my father's family in Israel, that I should be the king's son-in-law?"
9_18_18.MSG: David, embarrassed, answered, "Do you really mean that? I'm from a family of nobodies! I can't be son-in-law to the king."
9_18_18.NIV: But David said to Saul, “Who am I, and what is my family or my father’s clan in Israel, that I should become the king’s son-in-law?”
9_18_18.NRSVCE: David said to Saul, “Who am I and who are my kinsfolk, my father’s family in Israel, that I should be son-in-law to the king?”

9_18_19.NKJV: But it happened at the time when Merab, Saul’s daughter, should have been given to David, that she was given to Adriel the Meholathite as a wife.
9_18_19.KJV: But it came to pass at the time when Merab Saul's daughter should have been given to David, that she was given unto Adriel the Meholathite to wife.
9_18_19.ESV: But at the time when Merab, Saul's daughter, should have been given to David, she was given to Adriel the Meholathite for a wife.
9_18_19.NASB: So it came about at the time when Merab, Saul's daughter, should have been given to David, that she was given to Adriel the Meholathite for a wife.
9_18_19.NLT: So when the time came for Saul to give his daughter Merab in marriage to David, he gave her instead to Adriel, a man from Meholah.
9_18_19.WEB: But at the time when Merab, Saul’s daughter, should have been given to David, she was given to Adriel the Meholathite as wife.
9_18_19.CSB17: When it was time to give Saul’s daughter Merab to David, she was given to Adriel the Meholathite as a wife.
9_18_19.AMP: But at the time when Merab, Saul's daughter, should have been given to David, she was [instead] given to Adriel the Meholathite as a wife.
9_18_19.MSG: The wedding day was set, but as the time neared for Merab and David to be married, Saul reneged and married his daughter off to Adriel the Meholathite.
9_18_19.NIV: So when the time came for Merab, Saul’s daughter, to be given to David, she was given in marriage to Adriel of Meholah.
9_18_19.NRSVCE: But at the time when Saul’s daughter Merab should have been given to David, she was given to Adriel the Meholathite as a wife.

9_18_20.NKJV: Now Michal, Saul’s daughter, loved David. And they told Saul, and the thing pleased him.
9_18_20.KJV: And Michal Saul's daughter loved David: and they told Saul, and the thing pleased him. pleased him: Heb. was right in his eyes
9_18_20.ESV: Now Saul's daughter Michal loved David. And they told Saul, and the thing pleased him.
9_18_20.NASB: Now Michal, Saul's daughter, loved David. When they told Saul, the thing was agreeable to him.
9_18_20.NLT: In the meantime, Saul’s daughter Michal had fallen in love with David, and Saul was delighted when he heard about it.
9_18_20.WEB: Michal, Saul’s daughter, loved David; and they told Saul, and the thing pleased him.
9_18_20.CSB17: Now Saul’s daughter Michal loved David, and when it was reported to Saul, it pleased him.
9_18_20.AMP: Now Michal, Saul's daughter, loved David; and when they told Saul, it pleased him.
9_18_20.MSG: Meanwhile, Saul's daughter Michal was in love with David. When Saul was told of this, he rubbed his hands in anticipation. "Ah, a second chance. I'll use Michal as bait to get David out where the Philistines will make short work of him." So again he said to David, "You're going to be my son-in-law."
9_18_20.NIV: Now Saul’s daughter Michal was in love with David, and when they told Saul about it, he was pleased.
9_18_20.NRSVCE: Now Saul’s daughter Michal loved David. Saul was told, and the thing pleased him.

9_18_21.NKJV: So Saul said, “I will give her to him, that she may be a snare to him, and that the hand of the Philistines may be against him.” Therefore Saul said to David a second time, “You shall be my son-in-law today.”
9_18_21.KJV: And Saul said, I will give him her, that she may be a snare to him, and that the hand of the Philistines may be against him. Wherefore Saul said to David, Thou shalt this day be my son in law in the one of the twain.
9_18_21.ESV: Saul thought, “Let me give her to him, that she may be a snare for him and that the hand of the Philistines may be against him.” Therefore Saul said to David a second time, “You shall now be my son-in-law.”
9_18_21.NASB: Saul thought, 'I will give her to him that she may become a snare to him, and that the hand of the Philistines may be against him.' Therefore Saul said to David, 'For a second time you may be my son-in-law today.'
9_18_21.NLT: “Here’s another chance to see him killed by the Philistines!” Saul said to himself. But to David he said, “Today you have a second chance to become my son-in-law!”
9_18_21.WEB: Saul said, I will give her to him, that she may be a snare to him, and that the hand of the Philistines may be against him. Therefore Saul said to David, “You shall today be my son-in-law a second time.”
9_18_21.CSB17: “I’ll give her to him,” Saul thought. “She’ll be a trap for him, and the hand of the Philistines will be against him.” So Saul said to David a second time, “You can now be my son-in-law.”
9_18_21.AMP: Saul said, "I will give her to him so that she may become a snare (bad influence, source of trouble) to him, and that the hand of the Philistines may be against him." So Saul said to David for a second time, "You shall be my son-in-law today."
9_18_21.NIV: “I will give her to him,” he thought, “so that she may be a snare to him and so that the hand of the Philistines may be against him.” So Saul said to David, “Now you have a second opportunity to become my son-in-law.”
9_18_21.NRSVCE: Saul thought, “Let me give her to him that she may be a snare for him and that the hand of the Philistines may be against him.” Therefore Saul said to David a second time, “You shall now be my son-in-law.”

9_18_22.NKJV: And Saul commanded his servants, “Communicate with David secretly, and say, ‘Look, the king has delight in you, and all his servants love you. Now therefore, become the king’s son-in-law.’ ”
9_18_22.KJV: And Saul commanded his servants, saying, Commune with David secretly, and say, Behold, the king hath delight in thee, and all his servants love thee: now therefore be the king's son in law.
9_18_22.ESV: And Saul commanded his servants, “Speak to David in private and say, ‘Behold, the king has delight in you, and all his servants love you. Now then become the king's son-in-law.’”
9_18_22.NASB: Then Saul commanded his servants, 'Speak to David secretly, saying, 'Behold, the king delights in you, and all his servants love you; now therefore, become the king's son-in-law.''
9_18_22.NLT: Then Saul told his men to say to David, “The king really likes you, and so do we. Why don’t you accept the king’s offer and become his son-in-law?”
9_18_22.WEB: Saul commanded his servants, “Talk with David secretly, and say, ‘Behold, the king has delight in you, and all his servants love you. Now therefore be the king’s son-in-law.’”
9_18_22.CSB17: Saul then ordered his servants, “Speak to David in private and tell him, ‘Look, the king is pleased with you, and all his servants love you. Therefore, you should become the king’s son-in-law.’”
9_18_22.AMP: Then Saul commanded his servants, "Speak to David secretly, saying, 'Listen, the king delights in you, and all his servants love you; now then, become the king's son-in-law.'"
9_18_22.MSG: Saul ordered his servants, "Get David off by himself and tell him, 'The king is very taken with you, and everyone at court loves you. Go ahead, become the king's son-in-law!' "
9_18_22.NIV: Then Saul ordered his attendants: “Speak to David privately and say, ‘Look, the king is pleased with you, and his attendants all like you; now become his son-in-law.’”
9_18_22.NRSVCE: Saul commanded his servants, “Speak to David in private and say, ‘See, the king is delighted with you, and all his servants love you; now then, become the king’s son-in-law.’”

9_18_23.NKJV: So Saul’s servants spoke those words in the hearing of David. And David said, “Does it seem to you a light thing to be a king’s son-in-law, seeing I am a poor and lightly esteemed man?”
9_18_23.KJV: And Saul's servants spake those words in the ears of David. And David said, Seemeth it to you a light thing to be a king's son in law, seeing that I am a poor man, and lightly esteemed?
9_18_23.ESV: And Saul's servants spoke those words in the ears of David. And David said, “Does it seem to you a little thing to become the king's son-in-law, since I am a poor man and have no reputation?”
9_18_23.NASB: So Saul's servants spoke these words to David. But David said, 'Is it trivial in your sight to become the king's son-in-law, since I am a poor man and lightly esteemed?'
9_18_23.NLT: When Saul’s men said these things to David, he replied, “How can a poor man from a humble family afford the bride price for the daughter of a king?”

9_19_9.NLT: But one day when Saul was sitting at home, with spear in hand, the tormenting spirit from the LORD suddenly came upon him again. As David played his harp,

9_19_10.NKJV: Then Saul sought to pin David to the wall with the spear, but he slipped away from Saul’s presence; and he drove the spear into the wall. So David fled and escaped that night.

9_19_17.NKJV: Then Saul said to Michal, “Why have you deceived me like this, and sent my enemy away, so that he has escaped?” And Michal answered Saul, “He said to me, ‘Let me go! Why should I kill you?’ ”

9_19_17.NASB: So Saul said to Michal, 'Why have you deceived me like this and let my enemy go, so that he has escaped?' And Michal said to Saul, 'He said to me, 'Let me go! Why should I put you to death?''
