9_19_17.WEB: Saul said to Michal, “Why have you deceived me thus, and let my enemy go, so that he is escaped?” Michal answered Saul, “He said to me, ‘Let me go! Why should I kill you?’”

9_19_17.MSG: Saul stormed at Michal: "How could you play tricks on me like this? You sided with my enemy, and now he's gotten away!"Michal said, "He threatened me. He said, 'Help me out of here or I'll kill you.' "

9_19_17.NRSVCE: Saul said to Michal, “Why have you deceived me like this, and let my enemy go, so that he has escaped?” Michal answered Saul, “He said to me, ‘Let me go; why should I kill you?’”

9_19_19.CSB17: When it was reported to Saul that David was at Naioth in Ramah,

9_19_19.NIV: Word came to Saul: “David is in Naioth at Ramah”;
9_19_19.NRSVCE: Saul was told, “David is at Naioth in Ramah.”

9_19_20.NKJV: Then Saul sent messengers to take David. And when they saw the group of prophets prophesying, and Samuel standing as leader over them, the Spirit of God came upon the messengers of Saul, and they also prophesied.
9_19_20.KJV: And Saul sent messengers to take David: and when they saw the company of the prophets prophesying, and Samuel standing as appointed over them, the Spirit of God was upon the messengers of Saul, and they also prophesied.
9_19_20.ESV: Then Saul sent messengers to take David, and when they saw the company of the prophets prophesying, and Samuel standing as head over them, the Spirit of God came upon the messengers of Saul, and they also prophesied.
9_19_20.NASB: Then Saul sent messengers to take David, but when they saw the company of the prophets prophesying, with Samuel standing [and] presiding over them, the Spirit of God came upon the messengers of Saul; and they also prophesied.
9_19_20.NLT: he sent troops to capture him. But when they arrived and saw Samuel leading a group of prophets who were prophesying, the Spirit of God came upon Saul’s men, and they also began to prophesy.
9_19_20.WEB: Saul sent messengers to seize David: and when they saw the company of the prophets prophesying, and Samuel standing as head over them, God’s Spirit came on Saul’s messengers, and they also prophesied.
9_19_20.CSB17: he sent agents to seize David. However, when they saw the group of prophets prophesying with Samuel leading them, the Spirit of God came on Saul’s agents, and they also started prophesying.

9_19_20.MSG: He immediately sent his men to capture him. They saw a band of prophets prophesying with Samuel presiding over them. Before they knew it, the Spirit of God was on them, too, and they were ranting and raving right along with the prophets!
9_19_20.NIV: so he sent men to capture him. But when they saw a group of prophets prophesying, with Samuel standing there as their leader, the Spirit of God came upon Saul’s men and they also prophesied.

9_19_21.NKJV: And when Saul was told, he sent other messengers, and they prophesied likewise. Then Saul sent messengers again the third time, and they prophesied also.

9_19_21.NLT: When Saul heard what had happened, he sent other troops, but they, too, prophesied! The same thing happened a third time.
9_19_21.WEB: When Saul was told, he sent other messengers, and they also prophesied. Saul sent messengers again the third time, and they also prophesied.
9_19_21.CSB17: When they reported to Saul, he sent other agents, and they also began prophesying. So Saul tried again and sent a third group of agents, and even they began prophesying.

9_19_21.MSG: That was reported back to Saul, and he dispatched more men. They, too, were soon prophesying. So Saul tried a third time—a third set of men—and they ended up mindlessly raving as well!

9_19_21.NRSVCE: When Saul was told, he sent other messengers, and they also fell into a frenzy. Saul sent messengers again the third time, and they also fell into a frenzy.

9_19_23.NLT: But on the way to Naioth in Ramah the Spirit of God came even upon Saul, and he, too, began to prophesy all the way to Naioth!

9_19_23.CSB17: So he went to Naioth in Ramah. The Spirit of God also came on him, and as he walked along, he prophesied until he entered Naioth in Ramah.

9_19_23.MSG: As he headed out for Naioth in Ramah, the Spirit of God was on him, too. All the way to Naioth he was caught up in a babbling trance!

9_19_24.NKJV: And he also stripped off his clothes and prophesied before Samuel in like manner, and lay down naked all that day and all that night. Therefore they say, “Is Saul also among the prophets?”
9_19_24.KJV: And he stripped off his clothes also, and prophesied before Samuel in like manner, and lay down naked all that day and all that night. Wherefore they say, Is Saul also among the prophets? lay: Heb. fell
9_19_24.ESV: And he too stripped off his clothes, and he too prophesied before Samuel and lay naked all that day and all that night. Thus it is said, “Is Saul also among the prophets?”
9_19_24.NASB: He also stripped off his clothes, and he too prophesied before Samuel and lay down naked all that day and all that night. Therefore they say, 'Is Saul also among the prophets?'
9_19_24.NLT: He tore off his clothes and lay naked on the ground all day and all night, prophesying in the presence of Samuel. The people who were watching exclaimed, “What? Is even Saul a prophet?”
9_19_24.WEB: He also stripped off his clothes, and he also prophesied before Samuel, and lay down naked all that day and all that night. Therefore they say, “Is Saul also among the prophets?”
9_19_24.CSB17: Saul then removed his clothes and also prophesied before Samuel; he collapsed and lay naked all that day and all that night. That is why they say, “Is Saul also among the prophets?”
9_19_24.AMP: He also took off his [royal] robes [and armor] and prophesied before Samuel and lay down naked all that day and night. So they say, "Is Saul also among the prophets?"

9_20_1.NKJV: Then David fled from Naioth in Ramah, and went and said to Jonathan, “What have I done? What is my iniquity, and what is my sin before your father, that he seeks my life?”

9_20_1.ESV: Then David fled from Naioth in Ramah and came and said before Jonathan, “What have I done? What is my guilt? And what is my sin before your father, that he seeks my life?”
9_20_1.NASB: Then David fled from Naioth in Ramah, and came and said to Jonathan, 'What have I done? What is my iniquity? And what is my sin before your father, that he is seeking my life?'
9_20_1.NLT: David now fled from Naioth in Ramah and found Jonathan. “What have I done?” he exclaimed. “What is my crime? How have I offended your father that he is so determined to kill me?”

9_20_2.NKJV: So Jonathan said to him, “By no means! You shall not die! Indeed, my father will do nothing either great or small without first telling me. And why should my father hide this thing from me? It is not so!”
9_20_2.KJV: And he said unto him, God forbid; thou shalt not die: behold, my father will do nothing either great or small, but that he will shew it me: and why should my father hide this thing from me? it is not so. shew: Heb. uncover mine ear
9_20_2.ESV: And he said to him, “Far from it! You shall not die. Behold, my father does nothing either great or small without disclosing it to me. And why should my father hide this from me? It is not so.”
9_20_2.NASB: He said to him, 'Far from it, you shall not die. Behold, my father does nothing either great or small without disclosing it to me. So why should my father hide this thing from me? It is not so!'
9_20_2.NLT: “That’s not true!” Jonathan protested. “You’re not going to die. He always tells me everything he’s going to do, even the little things. I know my father wouldn’t hide something like this from me. It just isn’t so!”
9_20_2.WEB: He said to him, “Far from it; you will not die. Behold, my father does nothing either great or small, but that he discloses it to me. Why would my father hide this thing from me? It is not so.”
9_20_2.CSB17: Jonathan said to him, “No, you won’t die. Listen, my father doesn’t do anything, great or small, without telling me. So why would he hide this matter from me? This can’t be true.”
9_20_2.AMP: Jonathan said to him, "Far from it! You shall not die. My father does nothing important or insignificant without telling me. So why would he hide this thing from me? It is not so!"
9_20_2.MSG: "Nothing," said Jonathan. "You've done nothing wrong. And you're not going to die. Really, you're not! My father tells me everything. He does nothing, whether big or little, without confiding in me. So why would he do this behind my back? It can't be."
9_20_2.NIV: “Never!” Jonathan replied. “You are not going to die! Look, my father doesn’t do anything, great or small, without confiding in me. Why would he hide this from me? It’s not so!”
9_20_2.NRSVCE: He said to him, “Far from it! You shall not die. My father does nothing either great or small without disclosing it to me; and why should my father hide this from me? Never!”

9_20_3.NKJV: Then David took an oath again, and said, “Your father certainly knows that I have found favor in your eyes, and he has said, ‘Do not let Jonathan know this, lest he be grieved.’ But truly, as the LORD lives and as your soul lives, there is but a step between me and death.”
9_20_3.KJV: And David sware moreover, and said, Thy father certainly knoweth that I have found grace in thine eyes; and he saith, Let not Jonathan know this, lest he be grieved: but truly as the LORD liveth, and as thy soul liveth, there is but a step between me and death.
9_20_3.ESV: But David vowed again, saying, “Your father knows well that I have found favor in your eyes, and he thinks, ‘Do not let Jonathan know this, lest he be grieved.’ But truly, as the LORD lives and as your soul lives, there is but a step between me and death.”
9_20_3.NASB: Yet David vowed again, saying, 'Your father knows well that I have found favor in your sight, and he has said, 'Do not let Jonathan know this, or he will be grieved.' But truly as the LORD lives and as your soul lives, there is hardly a step between me and death.'
9_20_3.NLT: Then David took an oath before Jonathan and said, “Your father knows perfectly well about our friendship, so he has said to himself, ‘I won’t tell Jonathan — why should I hurt him?’ But I swear to you that I am only a step away from death! I swear it by the LORD and by your own soul!”
9_20_3.WEB: David swore moreover, and said, “Your father knows well that I have found favor in your eyes; and he says, ‘Don’t let Jonathan know this, lest he be grieved:’ but truly as Yahweh lives, and as your soul lives, there is but a step between me and death.”
9_20_3.CSB17: But David said, “Your father certainly knows that I have found favor with you. He has said, ‘Jonathan must not know of this, or else he will be grieved.’” David also swore, “As surely as the LORD lives and as you yourself live, there is but a step between me and death.”
9_20_3.AMP: But David vowed again, saying "Your father certainly knows that I have found favor in your sight, and he has said, 'Do not let Jonathan know this, or he will be worried.' But truly as the LORD lives and as your soul lives, there is hardly a step between me and death."
9_20_3.MSG: But David said, "Your father knows that we are the best of friends. So he says to himself, 'Jonathan must know nothing of this. If he does, he'll side with David.' But it's true—as sure as God lives, and as sure as you're alive before me right now—he's determined to kill me."
9_20_3.NIV: But David took an oath and said, “Your father knows very well that I have found favor in your eyes, and he has said to himself, ‘Jonathan must not know this or he will be grieved.’ Yet as surely as the Lord lives and as you live, there is only a step between me and death.”
9_20_3.NRSVCE: But David also swore, “Your father knows well that you like me; and he thinks, ‘Do not let Jonathan know this, or he will be grieved.’ But truly, as the LORD lives and as you yourself live, there is but a step between me and death.”

9_20_5.NKJV: And David said to Jonathan, “Indeed tomorrow is the New Moon, and I should not fail to sit with the king to eat. But let me go, that I may hide in the field until the third day at evening.

9_20_21.NKJV: and there I will send a lad, saying, ‘Go, find the arrows.’ If I expressly say to the lad, ‘Look, the arrows are on this side of you; get them and come’—then, as the LORD lives, there is safety for you and no harm.

9_20_30.NKJV: Then Saul’s anger was aroused against Jonathan, and he said to him, “You son of a perverse, rebellious woman! Do I not know that you have chosen the son of Jesse to your own shame and to the shame of your mother’s nakedness?

Thou: or, Thou perverse rebel: Heb. Son of perverse rebellion

9_21_5.NKJV: Then David answered the priest, and said to him, “Truly, women have been kept from us about three days since I came out. And the vessels of the young men are holy, and the bread is in effect common, even though it was consecrated in the vessel this day.”

9_21_5.ESV: And David answered the priest, “Truly women have been kept from us as always when I go on an expedition. The vessels of the young men are holy even when it is an ordinary journey. How much more today will their vessels be holy?”
9_21_5.NASB: David answered the priest and said to him, 'Surely women have been kept from us as previously when I set out and the vessels of the young men were holy, though it was an ordinary journey; how much more then today will their vessels [be holy]?'
9_21_5.NLT: “Don’t worry,” David replied. “I never allow my men to be with women when we are on a campaign. And since they stay clean even on ordinary trips, how much more on this one!”
9_21_5.WEB: David answered the priest, and said to him, “Truly, women have been kept from us as usual these three days. When I came out, the vessels of the young men were holy, though it was only a common journey. How much more then today shall their vessels be holy?”
9_21_5.CSB17: David answered him, “I swear that women are being kept from us, as always when I go out to battle. The young men’s bodies are consecrated even on an ordinary mission, so of course their bodies are consecrated today.”
9_21_5.AMP: David answered the priest, "Be assured that women have been kept from us in these three days since I set out, and the bodies of the young men were consecrated (ceremonially clean), although it was an ordinary (unconsecrated) journey; so how much more will their vessels be holy today?"
9_21_5.MSG: David said, "None of us has touched a woman. I always do it this way when I'm on a mission: My men abstain from sex. Even when it is an ordinary mission we do that—how much more on this holy mission."
9_21_5.NIV: David replied, “Indeed women have been kept from us, as usual whenever I set out. The men’s things are holy even on missions that are not holy. How much more so today!”
9_21_5.NRSVCE: David answered the priest, “Indeed women have been kept from us as always when I go on an expedition; the vessels of the young men are holy even when it is a common journey; how much more today will their vessels be holy?”

9_22_8.ESV: that all of you have conspired against me? No one discloses to me when my son makes a covenant with the son of Jesse. None of you is sorry for me or discloses to me that my son has stirred up my servant against me, to lie in wait, as at this day.”

9_22_8.NLT: Is that why you have conspired against me? For not one of you told me when my own son made a solemn pact with the son of Jesse. You’re not even sorry for me. Think of it! My own son — encouraging him to kill me, as he is trying to do this very day!”
9_22_8.WEB: that all of you have conspired against me, and there is no one who discloses to me when my son makes a treaty with the son of Jesse, and there is none of you who is sorry for me, or discloses to me that my son has stirred up my servant against me, to lie in wait, as it is today?”
9_22_8.CSB17: That’s why all of you have conspired against me! Nobody tells me when my own son makes a covenant with Jesse’s son. None of you cares about me or tells me that my son has stirred up my own servant to wait in ambush for me, as is the case today.”
9_22_8.AMP: For all of you have conspired against me so that no one informs me when my son [Jonathan] makes a covenant with the son of Jesse. None of you cares about me or informs me that my son has stirred up my servant against me to lie in ambush, as he does this day?"

9_23_13.NKJV: So David and his men, about six hundred, arose and departed from Keilah and went wherever they could go. Then it was told Saul that David had escaped from Keilah; so he halted the expedition.

9_23_26.NKJV: Then Saul went on one side of the mountain, and David and his men on the other side of the mountain. So David made haste to get away from Saul, for Saul and his men were encircling David and his men to take them.
9_23_26.KJV: And Saul went on this side of the mountain, and David and his men on that side of the mountain: and David made haste to get away for fear of Saul; for Saul and his men compassed David and his men round about to take them.
9_23_26.ESV: Saul went on one side of the mountain, and David and his men on the other side of the mountain. And David was hurrying to get away from Saul. As Saul and his men were closing in on David and his men to capture them,
9_23_26.NASB: Saul went on one side of the mountain, and David and his men on the other side of the mountain; and David was hurrying to get away from Saul, for Saul and his men were surrounding David and his men to seize them.
9_23_26.NLT: Saul and David were now on opposite sides of a mountain. Just as Saul and his men began to close in on David and his men,
9_23_26.WEB: Saul went on this side of the mountain, and David and his men on that side of the mountain; and David hurried to get away for fear of Saul; for Saul and his men surrounded David and his men to take them.
9_23_26.CSB17: Saul went along one side of the mountain and David and his men went along the other side. Even though David was hurrying to get away from Saul, Saul and his men were closing in on David and his men to capture them.
9_23_26.AMP: Saul went on one side of the mountain, and David and his men on the other side of the mountain. And David was hurrying to get away from Saul, for Saul and his men were surrounding David and his men to capture them.
9_23_26.MSG: Saul was on one side of the mountain, David and his men on the other. David was in full retreat, running, with Saul and his men closing in, about to get him.
9_23_26.NIV: Saul was going along one side of the mountain, and David and his men were on the other side, hurrying to get away from Saul. As Saul and his forces were closing in on David and his men to capture them,
9_23_26.NRSVCE: Saul went on one side of the mountain, and David and his men on the other side of the mountain. David was hurrying to get away from Saul, while Saul and his men were closing in on David and his men to capture them.

9_23_27.NKJV: But a messenger came to Saul, saying, “Hurry and come, for the Philistines have invaded the land!”
9_23_27.KJV: But there came a messenger unto Saul, saying, Haste thee, and come; for the Philistines have invaded the land. invaded: Heb. spread themselves upon, etc
9_23_27.ESV: a messenger came to Saul, saying, “Hurry and come, for the Philistines have made a raid against the land.”
9_23_27.NASB: But a messenger came to Saul, saying, 'Hurry and come, for the Philistines have made a raid on the land.'
9_23_27.NLT: an urgent message reached Saul that the Philistines were raiding Israel again.
9_23_27.WEB: But a messenger came to Saul, saying, “Hurry and come; for the Philistines have made a raid on the land!”
9_23_27.CSB17: Then a messenger came to Saul saying, “Come quickly, because the Philistines have raided the land!”
9_23_27.AMP: But a messenger came to Saul, saying, "Hurry and come, because the Philistines have attacked the land."
9_23_27.MSG: Just then a messenger came to Saul and said, "Hurry! Come back! The Philistines have just attacked the country!"
9_23_27.NIV: a messenger came to Saul, saying, “Come quickly! The Philistines are raiding the land.”
9_23_27.NRSVCE: Then a messenger came to Saul, saying, “Hurry and come; for the Philistines have made a raid on the land.”

9_23_28.NKJV: Therefore Saul returned from pursuing David, and went against the Philistines; so they called that place the Rock of Escape.
9_23_28.KJV: Wherefore Saul returned from pursuing after David, and went against the Philistines: therefore they called that place Selahammahlekoth. Selahammahlekoth: that is, The rock of divisions
9_23_28.ESV: So Saul returned from pursuing after David and went against the Philistines. Therefore that place was called the Rock of Escape.
9_23_28.NASB: So Saul returned from pursuing David and went to meet the Philistines; therefore they called that place the Rock of Escape.
9_23_28.NLT: So Saul quit chasing David and returned to fight the Philistines. Ever since that time, the place where David was camped has been called the Rock of Escape.
9_23_28.WEB: So Saul returned from pursuing David, and went against the Philistines. Therefore they called that place Sela Hammahlekoth.
9_23_28.CSB17: So Saul broke off his pursuit of David and went to engage the Philistines. Therefore, that place was named the Rock of Separation.
9_23_28.AMP: So Saul returned from pursuing David and went to meet the Philistines; therefore they called that place the Rock of Escape.
9_23_28.MSG: So Saul called off his pursuit of David and went back to deal with the Philistines. That's how that place got the name Narrow Escape.
9_23_28.NIV: Then Saul broke off his pursuit of David and went to meet the Philistines. That is why they call this place Sela Hammahlekoth.
9_23_28.NRSVCE: So Saul stopped pursuing David, and went against the Philistines; therefore that place was called the Rock of Escape.

9_23_29.NKJV: Then David went up from there and dwelt in strongholds at En Gedi.
9_23_29.KJV: And David went up from thence, and dwelt in strong holds at Engedi.
9_23_29.ESV: And David went up from there and lived in the strongholds of Engedi.
9_23_29.NASB: David went up from there and stayed in the strongholds of Engedi.
9_23_29.NLT: David then went to live in the strongholds of En-gedi.
9_23_29.WEB: David went up from there, and lived in the strongholds of En Gedi.
9_23_29.CSB17: From there David went up and stayed in the strongholds of En-gedi.
9_23_29.AMP: David went up from there and stayed in the strongholds of Engedi.
9_23_29.MSG: David left there and camped out in the caves and canyons of En Gedi.
9_23_29.NIV: And David went up from there and lived in the strongholds of En Gedi.
9_23_29.NRSVCE: David then went up from there, and lived in the strongholds of En-gedi.

9_24_1.NKJV: Now it happened, when Saul had returned from following the Philistines, that it was told him, saying, “Take note! David is in the Wilderness of En Gedi.”
9_24_1.KJV: And it came to pass, when Saul was returned from following the Philistines, that it was told him, saying, Behold, David is in the wilderness of Engedi. following: Heb. after
9_24_1.ESV: When Saul returned from following the Philistines, he was told, “Behold, David is in the wilderness of Engedi.”
9_24_1.NASB: Now when Saul returned from pursuing the Philistines, he was told, saying, 'Behold, David is in the wilderness of Engedi.'
9_24_1.NLT: After Saul returned from fighting the Philistines, he was told that David had gone into the wilderness of En-gedi.
9_24_1.WEB: When Saul had returned from following the Philistines, he was told, “Behold, David is in the wilderness of En Gedi.”
9_24_1.CSB17: When Saul returned from pursuing the Philistines, he was told, “David is in the wilderness near En-gedi.”
9_24_1.AMP: When Saul returned from following the Philistines, he was told, "Behold, David is in the Wilderness of Engedi."
9_24_1.MSG: When Saul came back after dealing with the Philistines, he was told, "David is now in the wilderness of En Gedi."
9_24_1.NIV: David Spares Saul’s LifeAfter Saul returned from pursuing the Philistines, he was told, “David is in the Desert of En Gedi.”
9_24_1.NRSVCE: When Saul returned from following the Philistines, he was told, “David is in the wilderness of En-gedi.”

9_24_2.NKJV: Then Saul took three thousand chosen men from all Israel, and went to seek David and his men on the Rocks of the Wild Goats.
9_24_2.KJV: Then Saul took three thousand chosen men out of all Israel, and went to seek David and his men upon the rocks of the wild goats.
9_24_2.ESV: Then Saul took three thousand chosen men out of all Israel and went to seek David and his men in front of the Wildgoats' Rocks.
9_24_2.NASB: Then Saul took three thousand chosen men from all Israel and went to seek David and his men in front of the Rocks of the Wild Goats.
9_24_2.NLT: So Saul chose 3,000 elite troops from all Israel and went to search for David and his men near the rocks of the wild goats.
9_24_2.WEB: Then Saul took three thousand chosen men out of all Israel, and went to seek David and his men on the rocks of the wild goats.
9_24_2.CSB17: So Saul took three thousand of Israel’s fit young men and went to look for David and his men in front of the Rocks of the Wild Goats.
9_24_2.AMP: Then Saul took three thousand chosen men out of all Israel and went to search for David and his men in front of the Rocks of the Wild Goats.
9_24_2.MSG: Saul took three companies—the best he could find in all Israel—and set out in search of David and his men in the region of Wild Goat Rocks.
9_24_2.NIV: So Saul took three thousand chosen men from all Israel and set out to look for David and his men near the Crags of the Wild Goats.
9_24_2.NRSVCE: Then Saul took three thousand chosen men out of all Israel, and went to look for David and his men in the direction of the Rocks of the Wild Goats.

9_24_3.NKJV: So he came to the sheepfolds by the road, where there was a cave; and Saul went in to attend to his needs. ( David and his men were staying in the recesses of the cave.)
9_24_3.KJV: And he came to the sheepcotes by the way, where was a cave; and Saul went in to cover his feet: and David and his men remained in the sides of the cave.
9_24_3.ESV: And he came to the sheepfolds by the way, where there was a cave, and Saul went in to relieve himself. Now David and his men were sitting in the innermost parts of the cave.
9_24_3.NASB: He came to the sheepfolds on the way, where there [was] a cave; and Saul went in to relieve himself. Now David and his men were sitting in the inner recesses of the cave.
9_24_3.NLT: At the place where the road passes some sheepfolds, Saul went into a cave to relieve himself. But as it happened, David and his men were hiding farther back in that very cave!
9_24_3.WEB: He came to the sheep pens by the way, where there was a cave; and Saul went in to relieve himself. Now David and his men were staying in the innermost parts of the cave.
9_24_3.CSB17: When Saul came to the sheep pens along the road, a cave was there, and he went in to relieve himself. David and his men were staying in the recesses of the cave,
9_24_3.AMP: On the way he came to the sheepfolds where there was a cave; and Saul went in to relieve himself. Now David and his men were sitting in the cave's innermost recesses.
9_24_3.MSG: He came to some sheep pens along the road. There was a cave there and Saul went in to relieve himself. David and his men were huddled far back in the same cave.
9_24_3.NIV: He came to the sheep pens along the way; a cave was there, and Saul went in to relieve himself. David and his men were far back in the cave.
9_24_3.NRSVCE: He came to the sheepfolds beside the road, where there was a cave; and Saul went in to relieve himself. Now David and his men were sitting in the innermost parts of the cave.

9_24_4.NKJV: Then the men of David said to him, “This is the day of which the LORD said to you, ‘Behold, I will deliver your enemy into your hand, that you may do to him as it seems good to you.’ ” And David arose and secretly cut off a corner of Saul’s robe.
9_24_4.KJV: And the men of David said unto him, Behold the day of which the LORD said unto thee, Behold, I will deliver thine enemy into thine hand, that thou mayest do to him as it shall seem good unto thee. Then David arose, and cut off the skirt of Saul's robe privily. Saul's: Heb. the robe which was Saul's
9_24_4.ESV: And the men of David said to him, “Here is the day of which the LORD said to you, ‘Behold, I will give your enemy into your hand, and you shall do to him as it shall seem good to you.’” Then David arose and stealthily cut off a corner of Saul's robe.
9_24_4.NASB: The men of David said to him, 'Behold, [this is] the day of which the LORD said to you, 'Behold; I am about to give your enemy into your hand, and you shall do to him as it seems good to you.'' Then David arose and cut off the edge of Saul's robe secretly.
9_24_4.NLT: “Now’s your opportunity!” David’s men whispered to him. “Today the LORD is telling you, ‘I will certainly put your enemy into your power, to do with as you wish.’” So David crept forward and cut off a piece of the hem of Saul’s robe.
9_24_4.WEB: David’s men said to him, “Behold, the day of which Yahweh said to you, ‘Behold, I will deliver your enemy into your hand, and you shall do to him as it shall seem good to you.’” Then David arose, and cut off the skirt of Saul’s robe secretly.
9_24_4.CSB17: so they said to him, “Look, this is the day the LORD told you about: ‘I will hand your enemy over to you so you can do to him whatever you desire.’” Then David got up and secretly cut off the corner of Saul’s robe.
9_24_4.AMP: David's men said to him, "Behold, this is the day of which the LORD said to you, 'Behold, I will hand over your enemy to you, and you shall do to him as seems good to you.'" Then David arose [in the darkness] and stealthily cut off the hem (edge) of Saul's robe.
9_24_4.MSG: David's men whispered to him, "Can you believe it? This is the day God was talking about when he said, 'I'll put your enemy in your hands. You can do whatever you want with him.' " Quiet as a cat, David crept up and cut off a piece of Saul's royal robe.
9_24_4.NIV: The men said, “This is the day the Lord spoke of when he said to you, ‘I will give your enemy into your hands for you to deal with as you wish.’” Then David crept up unnoticed and cut off a corner of Saul’s robe.
9_24_4.NRSVCE: The men of David said to him, “Here is the day of which the LORD said to you, ‘I will give your enemy into your hand, and you shall do to him as it seems good to you.’” Then David went and stealthily cut off a corner of Saul’s cloak.

9_24_5.NKJV: Now it happened afterward that David’s heart troubled him because he had cut Saul’s robe.
9_24_5.KJV: And it came to pass afterward, that David's heart smote him, because he had cut off Saul's skirt.
9_24_5.ESV: And afterward David's heart struck him, because he had cut off a corner of Saul's robe.
9_24_5.NASB: It came about afterward that David's conscience bothered him because he had cut off the edge of Saul's [robe].
9_24_5.NLT: But then David’s conscience began bothering him because he had cut Saul’s robe.
9_24_5.WEB: Afterward, David’s heart struck him, because he had cut off Saul’s skirt.
9_24_5.CSB17: Afterward, David’s conscience bothered him because he had cut off the corner of Saul’s robe.
9_24_5.AMP: Afterward, David's conscience bothered him because he had cut off the hem of Saul's robe.
9_24_5.NIV: Afterward, David was conscience-stricken for having cut off a corner of his robe.
9_24_5.NRSVCE: Afterward David was stricken to the heart because he had cut off a corner of Saul’s cloak.

9_24_6.NKJV: And he said to his men, “The LORD forbid that I should do this thing to my master, the LORD’s anointed, to stretch out my hand against him, seeing he is the anointed of the LORD.”
9_24_6.KJV: And he said unto his men, The LORD forbid that I should do this thing unto my master, the LORD'S anointed, to stretch forth mine hand against him, seeing he is the anointed of the LORD.
9_24_6.ESV: He said to his men, “The LORD forbid that I should do this thing to my lord, the LORD's anointed, to put out my hand against him, seeing he is the LORD's anointed.”
9_24_6.NASB: So he said to his men, 'Far be it from me because of the LORD that I should do this thing to my lord, the LORD'S anointed, to stretch out my hand against him, since he is the LORD'S anointed.'
9_24_6.NLT: He said to his men, “The LORD forbid that I should do this to my lord the king. I shouldn’t attack the LORD’s anointed one, for the LORD himself has chosen him.”
9_24_6.WEB: He said to his men, “Yahweh forbid that I should do this thing to my lord, Yahweh’s anointed, to stretch out my hand against him, since he is Yahweh’s anointed.”
9_24_6.CSB17: He said to his men, “I swear before the LORD: I would never do such a thing to my lord, the LORD’s anointed. I will never lift my hand against him, since he is the LORD’s anointed.”
9_24_6.AMP: He said to his men, "The LORD forbid that I should do this thing to my master, the Lord's anointed, to put out my hand against him, since he is the anointed of the LORD."
9_24_6.MSG: He said to his men, "God forbid that I should have done this to my master, God's anointed, that I should so much as raise a finger against him. He's God's anointed!"
9_24_6.NIV: He said to his men, “The Lord forbid that I should do such a thing to my master, the Lord’s anointed, or lift my hand against him; for he is the anointed of the Lord.”
9_24_6.NRSVCE: He said to his men, “The LORD forbid that I should do this thing to my lord, the LORD’s anointed, to raise my hand against him; for he is the LORD’s anointed.”

9_24_7.NKJV: So David restrained his servants with these words, and did not allow them to rise against Saul. And Saul got up from the cave and went on his way.
9_24_7.KJV: So David stayed his servants with these words, and suffered them not to rise against Saul. But Saul rose up out of the cave, and went on his way. stayed: Heb. cut off
9_24_7.ESV: So David persuaded his men with these words and did not permit them to attack Saul. And Saul rose up and left the cave and went on his way.
9_24_7.NASB: David persuaded his men with [these] words and did not allow them to rise up against Saul. And Saul arose, left the cave, and went on [his] way.
9_24_7.NLT: So David restrained his men and did not let them kill Saul. After Saul had left the cave and gone on his way,
9_24_7.WEB: So David checked his men with these words, and didn’t allow them to rise against Saul. Saul rose up out of the cave, and went on his way.
9_24_7.CSB17: With these words David persuaded his men, and he did not let them rise up against Saul. Then Saul left the cave and went on his way.
9_24_7.AMP: So David strongly rebuked his men with these words and did not let them rise up against Saul. Saul got up, left the cave and went on his way.
9_24_7.MSG: David held his men in check with these words and wouldn't let them pounce on Saul. Saul got up, left the cave, and went on down the road.
9_24_7.NIV: With these words David rebuked his men and did not allow them to attack Saul. And Saul left the cave and went his way.
9_24_7.NRSVCE: So David scolded his men severely and did not permit them to attack Saul. Then Saul got up and left the cave, and went on his way.

9_24_8.NKJV: David also arose afterward, went out of the cave, and called out to Saul, saying, “My lord the king!” And when Saul looked behind him, David stooped with his face to the earth, and bowed down.
9_24_8.KJV: David also arose afterward, and went out of the cave, and cried after Saul, saying, My lord the king. And when Saul looked behind him, David stooped with his face to the earth, and bowed himself.
9_24_8.ESV: Afterward David also arose and went out of the cave, and called after Saul, “My lord the king!” And when Saul looked behind him, David bowed with his face to the earth and paid homage.
9_24_8.NASB: Now afterward David arose and went out of the cave and called after Saul, saying, 'My lord the king!' And when Saul looked behind him, David bowed with his face to the ground and prostrated himself.
9_24_8.NLT: David came out and shouted after him, “My lord the king!” And when Saul looked around, David bowed low before him.
9_24_8.WEB: David also arose afterward, and went out of the cave, and cried after Saul, saying, “My lord the king!” When Saul looked behind him, David bowed with his face to the earth, and showed respect.
9_24_8.CSB17: After that, David got up, went out of the cave, and called to Saul, “My lord the king!” When Saul looked behind him, David knelt low with his face to the ground and paid homage.
9_24_8.AMP: Then David also got up afterward and went out of the cave and called after Saul, saying, "My lord the king!" And when Saul looked behind him, David bowed with his face to the ground and lay himself face down.
9_24_8.MSG: Then David stood at the mouth of the cave and called to Saul, "My master! My king!" Saul looked back. David fell to his knees and bowed in reverence.
9_24_8.NIV: Then David went out of the cave and called out to Saul, “My lord the king!” When Saul looked behind him, David bowed down and prostrated himself with his face to the ground.
9_24_8.NRSVCE: Afterwards David also rose up and went out of the cave and called after Saul, “My lord the king!” When Saul looked behind him, David bowed with his face to the ground, and did obeisance.

9_24_9.NKJV: And David said to Saul: “Why do you listen to the words of men who say, ‘Indeed David seeks your harm’?
9_24_9.KJV: And David said to Saul, Wherefore hearest thou men's words, saying, Behold, David seeketh thy hurt?
9_24_9.ESV: And David said to Saul, “Why do you listen to the words of men who say, ‘Behold, David seeks your harm’?
9_24_9.NASB: David said to Saul, 'Why do you listen to the words of men, saying, 'Behold, David seeks to harm you'?
9_24_9.NLT: Then he shouted to Saul, “Why do you listen to the people who say I am trying to harm you?
9_24_9.WEB: David said to Saul, “Why do you listen to men’s words, saying, ‘Behold, David seeks to harm you?’
9_24_9.CSB17: David said to Saul, “Why do you listen to the words of people who say, ‘Look, David intends to harm you’?
9_24_9.AMP: David said to Saul, "Why do you listen to the words of men who say, 'David seeks to harm you?'
9_24_9.MSG: He called out, "Why do you listen to those who say 'David is out to get you'?
9_24_9.NIV: He said to Saul, “Why do you listen when men say, ‘David is bent on harming you’?
9_24_9.NRSVCE: David said to Saul, “Why do you listen to the words of those who say, ‘David seeks to do you harm’?

9_24_10.NKJV: Look, this day your eyes have seen that the LORD delivered you today into my hand in the cave, and someone urged me to kill you. But my eye spared you, and I said, ‘I will not stretch out my hand against my lord, for he is the LORD’s anointed.’
9_24_10.KJV: Behold, this day thine eyes have seen how that the LORD had delivered thee to day into mine hand in the cave: and some bade me kill thee: but mine eye spared thee; and I said, I will not put forth mine hand against my lord; for he is the LORD'S anointed.
9_24_10.ESV: Behold, this day your eyes have seen how the LORD gave you today into my hand in the cave. And some told me to kill you, but I spared you. I said, ‘I will not put out my hand against my lord, for he is the LORD's anointed.’
9_24_10.NASB: 'Behold, this day your eyes have seen that the LORD had given you today into my hand in the cave, and some said to kill you, but [my eye] had pity on you; and I said, 'I will not stretch out my hand against my lord, for he is the LORD'S anointed.'
9_24_10.NLT: This very day you can see with your own eyes it isn’t true. For the LORD placed you at my mercy back there in the cave. Some of my men told me to kill you, but I spared you. For I said, ‘I will never harm the king — he is the LORD’s anointed one.’
9_24_10.WEB: Behold, today your eyes have seen how Yahweh had delivered you today into my hand in the cave. Some urged me to kill you; but I spared you; and I said, I will not stretch out my hand against my lord; for he is Yahweh’s anointed.
9_24_10.CSB17: You can see with your own eyes that the LORD handed you over to me today in the cave. Someone advised me to kill you, but I took pity on you and said: I won’t lift my hand against my lord, since he is the LORD’s anointed.
9_24_10.AMP: Behold, your eyes have seen today how the LORD had given you into my hand in the cave. Some told me to kill you, but I spared you; I said, 'I will not reach out my hand against my lord, for he is the Lord's anointed.'
9_24_10.MSG: This very day with your very own eyes you have seen that just now in the cave God put you in my hands. My men wanted me to kill you, but I wouldn't do it. I told them that I won't lift a finger against my master—he's God's anointed.
9_24_10.NIV: This day you have seen with your own eyes how the Lord delivered you into my hands in the cave. Some urged me to kill you, but I spared you; I said, ‘I will not lift my hand against my master, because he is the Lord’s anointed.’
9_24_10.NRSVCE: This very day your eyes have seen how the LORD gave you into my hand in the cave; and some urged me to kill you, but I spared you. I said, ‘I will not raise my hand against my lord; for he is the LORD’s anointed.’

9_24_11.NKJV: Moreover, my father, see! Yes, see the corner of your robe in my hand! For in that I cut off the corner of your robe, and did not kill you, know and see that there is neither evil nor rebellion in my hand, and I have not sinned against you. Yet you hunt my life to take it.
9_24_11.KJV: Moreover, my father, see, yea, see the skirt of thy robe in my hand: for in that I cut off the skirt of thy robe, and killed thee not, know thou and see that there is neither evil nor transgression in mine hand, and I have not sinned against thee; yet thou huntest my soul to take it.
9_24_11.ESV: See, my father, see the corner of your robe in my hand. For by the fact that I cut off the corner of your robe and did not kill you, you may know and see that there is no wrong or treason in my hands. I have not sinned against you, though you hunt my life to take it.
9_24_11.NASB: 'Now, my father, see! Indeed, see the edge of your robe in my hand! For in that I cut off the edge of your robe and did not kill you, know and perceive that there is no evil or rebellion in my hands, and I have not sinned against you, though you are lying in wait for my life to take it.
9_24_11.NLT: Look, my father, at what I have in my hand. It is a piece of the hem of your robe! I cut it off, but I didn’t kill you. This proves that I am not trying to harm you and that I have not sinned against you, even though you have been hunting for me to kill me.
9_24_11.WEB: Moreover, my father, behold, yes, see the skirt of your robe in my hand; for in that I cut off the skirt of your robe, and didn’t kill you, know and see that there is neither evil nor disobedience in my hand, and I have not sinned against you, though you hunt for my life to take it.
9_24_11.CSB17: Look, my father! Look at the corner of your robe in my hand, for I cut it off, but I didn’t kill you. Recognize that I’ve committed no crime or rebellion. I haven’t sinned against you even though you are hunting me down to take my life.
9_24_11.AMP: Look, my father! Indeed, see the hem of your robe in my hand! Since I cut off the hem of your robe and did not kill you, know and understand [without question] that there is no evil or treason in my hands. I have not sinned against you, though you are lying in wait to take my life.
9_24_11.MSG: Oh, my father, look at this, look at this piece that I cut from your robe. I could have cut you—killed you!—but I didn't. Look at the evidence! I'm not against you. I'm no rebel. I haven't sinned against you, and yet you're hunting me down to kill me.
9_24_11.NIV: See, my father, look at this piece of your robe in my hand! I cut off the corner of your robe but did not kill you. Now understand and recognize that I am not guilty of wrongdoing or rebellion. I have not wronged you, but you are hunting me down to take my life.
9_24_11.NRSVCE: See, my father, see the corner of your cloak in my hand; for by the fact that I cut off the corner of your cloak, and did not kill you, you may know for certain that there is no wrong or treason in my hands. I have not sinned against you, though you are hunting me to take my life.

9_24_12.NKJV: Let the LORD judge between you and me, and let the LORD avenge me on you. But my hand shall not be against you.
9_24_12.KJV: The LORD judge between me and thee, and the LORD avenge me of thee: but mine hand shall not be upon thee.
9_24_12.ESV: May the LORD judge between me and you, may the LORD avenge me against you, but my hand shall not be against you.
9_24_12.NASB: 'May the LORD judge between you and me, and may the LORD avenge me on you; but my hand shall not be against you.
9_24_12.NLT: “May the LORD judge between us. Perhaps the LORD will punish you for what you are trying to do to me, but I will never harm you.
9_24_12.WEB: May Yahweh judge between me and you, and may Yahweh avenge me of you; but my hand will not be on you.
9_24_12.CSB17: “May the LORD judge between me and you, and may the LORD take vengeance on you for me, but my hand will never be against you.
9_24_12.AMP: May the LORD judge between me and you; and may the LORD avenge me on you; but my hand shall not be against you.
9_24_12.MSG: Let's decide which of us is in the right. God may avenge me, but it is in his hands, not mine.
9_24_12.NIV: May the Lord judge between you and me. And may the Lord avenge the wrongs you have done to me, but my hand will not touch you.
9_24_12.NRSVCE: May the LORD judge between me and you! May the LORD avenge me on you; but my hand shall not be against you.

9_24_13.NKJV: As the proverb of the ancients says, ‘Wickedness proceeds from the wicked.’ But my hand shall not be against you.
9_24_13.KJV: As saith the proverb of the ancients, Wickedness proceedeth from the wicked: but mine hand shall not be upon thee.
9_24_13.ESV: As the proverb of the ancients says, ‘Out of the wicked comes wickedness.’ But my hand shall not be against you.
9_24_13.NASB: 'As the proverb of the ancients says, 'Out of the wicked comes forth wickedness'; but my hand shall not be against you.
9_24_13.NLT: As that old proverb says, ‘From evil people come evil deeds.’ So you can be sure I will never harm you.
9_24_13.WEB: As the proverb of the ancients says, ‘Out of the wicked comes wickedness;’ but my hand will not be on you.
9_24_13.CSB17: As the old proverb says, ‘Wickedness comes from wicked people.’ My hand will never be against you.
9_24_13.AMP: As the proverb of the ancients says, 'Out of the wicked comes wickedness'; but my hand shall not be against you.
9_24_13.MSG: An old proverb says, 'Evil deeds come from evil people.' So be assured that my hand won't touch you.
9_24_13.NIV: As the old saying goes, ‘From evildoers come evil deeds,’ so my hand will not touch you.
9_24_13.NRSVCE: As the ancient proverb says, ‘Out of the wicked comes forth wickedness’; but my hand shall not be against you.

9_24_14.NKJV: After whom has the king of Israel come out? Whom do you pursue? A dead dog? A flea?
9_24_14.KJV: After whom is the king of Israel come out? after whom dost thou pursue? after a dead dog, after a flea.
9_24_14.ESV: After whom has the king of Israel come out? After whom do you pursue? After a dead dog! After a flea!
9_24_14.NASB: 'After whom has the king of Israel come out? Whom are you pursuing? A dead dog, a single flea?
9_24_14.NLT: Who is the king of Israel trying to catch anyway? Should he spend his time chasing one who is as worthless as a dead dog or a single flea?
9_24_14.WEB: Against whom has the king of Israel come out? Whom do you pursue? A dead dog? A flea?
9_24_14.CSB17: Who has the king of Israel come after? What are you chasing after? A dead dog? A single flea?
9_24_14.AMP: After whom has the king of Israel come out? Whom do you pursue [with three thousand men]? A dead dog, a single flea?
9_24_14.MSG: "What does the king of Israel think he's doing? Who do you think you're chasing? A dead dog? A flea?
9_24_14.NIV: “Against whom has the king of Israel come out? Whom are you pursuing? A dead dog? A flea?
9_24_14.NRSVCE: Against whom has the king of Israel come out? Whom do you pursue? A dead dog? A single flea?

9_24_15.NKJV: Therefore let the LORD be judge, and judge between you and me, and see and plead my case, and deliver me out of your hand.”
9_24_15.KJV: The LORD therefore be judge, and judge between me and thee, and see, and plead my cause, and deliver me out of thine hand. deliver: Heb. judge
9_24_15.ESV: May the LORD therefore be judge and give sentence between me and you, and see to it and plead my cause and deliver me from your hand.”
9_24_15.NASB: 'The LORD therefore be judge and decide between you and me; and may He see and plead my cause and deliver me from your hand.'
9_24_15.NLT: May the LORD therefore judge which of us is right and punish the guilty one. He is my advocate, and he will rescue me from your power!”
9_24_15.WEB: May Yahweh therefore be judge, and give sentence between me and you, and see, and plead my cause, and deliver me out of your hand.”
9_24_15.CSB17: May the LORD be judge and decide between you and me. May he take notice and plead my case and deliver me from you.”
9_24_15.AMP: May the LORD be the judge and render judgment between me and you; and may He see and plead my cause and vindicate me by saving me from your hand."
9_24_15.MSG: God is our judge. He'll decide who is right. Oh, that he would look down right now, decide right now—and set me free of you!"
9_24_15.NIV: May the Lord be our judge and decide between us. May he consider my cause and uphold it; may he vindicate me by delivering me from your hand.”
9_24_15.NRSVCE: May the LORD therefore be judge, and give sentence between me and you. May he see to it, and plead my cause, and vindicate me against you.”

9_24_16.NKJV: So it was, when David had finished speaking these words to Saul, that Saul said, “Is this your voice, my son David?” And Saul lifted up his voice and wept.
9_24_16.KJV: And it came to pass, when David had made an end of speaking these words unto Saul, that Saul said, Is this thy voice, my son David? And Saul lifted up his voice, and wept.
9_24_16.ESV: As soon as David had finished speaking these words to Saul, Saul said, “Is this your voice, my son David?” And Saul lifted up his voice and wept.
9_24_16.NASB: When David had finished speaking these words to Saul, Saul said, 'Is this your voice, my son David?' Then Saul lifted up his voice and wept.
9_24_16.NLT: When David had finished speaking, Saul called back, “Is that really you, my son David?” Then he began to cry.
9_24_16.WEB: It came to pass, when David had finished speaking these words to Saul, that Saul said, “Is that your voice, my son David?” Saul lifted up his voice, and wept.
9_24_16.CSB17: When David finished saying these things to him, Saul replied, “Is that your voice, David my son?” Then Saul wept aloud
9_24_16.AMP: When David had finished saying these words to Saul, Saul said, "Is this your voice, my son David?" Then Saul raised his voice and wept.
9_24_16.MSG: When David had finished saying all this, Saul said, "Can this be the voice of my son David?" and he wept in loud sobs.
9_24_16.NIV: When David finished saying this, Saul asked, “Is that your voice, David my son?” And he wept aloud.
9_24_16.NRSVCE: When David had finished speaking these words to Saul, Saul said, “Is this your voice, my son David?” Saul lifted up his voice and wept.

9_24_17.NKJV: Then he said to David: “You are more righteous than I; for you have rewarded me with good, whereas I have rewarded you with evil.
9_24_17.KJV: And he said to David, Thou art more righteous than I: for thou hast rewarded me good, whereas I have rewarded thee evil.
9_24_17.ESV: He said to David, “You are more righteous than I, for you have repaid me good, whereas I have repaid you evil.
9_24_17.NASB: He said to David, 'You are more righteous than I; for you have dealt well with me, while I have dealt wickedly with you.
9_24_17.NLT: And he said to David, “You are a better man than I am, for you have repaid me good for evil.
9_24_17.WEB: He said to David, “You are more righteous than I; for you have done good to me, whereas I have done evil to you.
9_24_17.CSB17: and said to David, “You are more righteous than I, for you have done what is good to me though I have done what is evil to you.
9_24_17.AMP: He said to David, "You are more righteous and upright [in God's eyes] than I; for you have done good to me, but I have done evil to you.
9_24_17.MSG: "You're the one in the right, not me," he continued. "You've heaped good on me; I've dumped evil on you.
9_24_17.NIV: “You are more righteous than I,” he said. “You have treated me well, but I have treated you badly.
9_24_17.NRSVCE: He said to David, “You are more righteous than I; for you have repaid me good, whereas I have repaid you evil.

9_24_18.NKJV: And you have shown this day how you have dealt well with me; for when the LORD delivered me into your hand, you did not kill me.
9_24_18.KJV: And thou hast shewed this day how that thou hast dealt well with me: forasmuch as when the LORD had delivered me into thine hand, thou killedst me not. delivered: Heb. shut up
9_24_18.ESV: And you have declared this day how you have dealt well with me, in that you did not kill me when the LORD put me into your hands.
9_24_18.NASB: 'You have declared today that you have done good to me, that the LORD delivered me into your hand and [yet] you did not kill me.
9_24_18.NLT: Yes, you have been amazingly kind to me today, for when the LORD put me in a place where you could have killed me, you didn’t do it.
9_24_18.WEB: You have declared today how you have dealt well with me, because when Yahweh had delivered me up into your hand, you didn’t kill me.
9_24_18.CSB17: You yourself have told me today what good you did for me: when the LORD handed me over to you, you didn’t kill me.
9_24_18.AMP: You have declared today the good that you have done to me, for when the LORD put me into your hand, you did not kill me.
9_24_18.MSG: And now you've done it again—treated me generously. God put me in your hands and you didn't kill me.
9_24_18.NIV: You have just now told me of the good you did to me; the Lord delivered me into your hands, but you did not kill me.
9_24_18.NRSVCE: Today you have explained how you have dealt well with me, in that you did not kill me when the LORD put me into your hands.

9_24_19.NKJV: For if a man finds his enemy, will he let him get away safely? Therefore may the LORD reward you with good for what you have done to me this day.
9_24_19.KJV: For if a man find his enemy, will he let him go well away? wherefore the LORD reward thee good for that thou hast done unto me this day.
9_24_19.ESV: For if a man finds his enemy, will he let him go away safe? So may the LORD reward you with good for what you have done to me this day.
9_24_19.NASB: 'For if a man finds his enemy, will he let him go away safely? May the LORD therefore reward you with good in return for what you have done to me this day.
9_24_19.NLT: Who else would let his enemy get away when he had him in his power? May the LORD reward you well for the kindness you have shown me today.
9_24_19.WEB: For if a man finds his enemy, will he let him go away unharmed? Therefore may Yahweh reward you good for that which you have done to me today.
9_24_19.CSB17: When a man finds his enemy, does he let him go unharmed? May the LORD repay you with good for what you’ve done for me today.
9_24_19.AMP: For if a man finds his enemy, will he let him go away unharmed? So may the LORD reward you with good in return for what you have done for me this day.
9_24_19.MSG: Why? When a man meets his enemy, does he send him down the road with a blessing? May God give you a bonus of blessings for what you've done for me today!
9_24_19.NIV: When a man finds his enemy, does he let him get away unharmed? May the Lord reward you well for the way you treated me today.
9_24_19.NRSVCE: For who has ever found an enemy, and sent the enemy safely away? So may the LORD reward you with good for what you have done to me this day.

9_24_20.NKJV: And now I know indeed that you shall surely be king, and that the kingdom of Israel shall be established in your hand.
9_24_20.KJV: And now, behold, I know well that thou shalt surely be king, and that the kingdom of Israel shall be established in thine hand.
9_24_20.ESV: And now, behold, I know that you shall surely be king, and that the kingdom of Israel shall be established in your hand.
9_24_20.NASB: 'Now, behold, I know that you will surely be king, and that the kingdom of Israel will be established in your hand.
9_24_20.NLT: And now I realize that you are surely going to be king, and that the kingdom of Israel will flourish under your rule.
9_24_20.WEB: Now, behold, I know that you will surely be king, and that the kingdom of Israel will be established in your hand.
9_24_20.CSB17: “Now I know for certain you will be king, and the kingdom of Israel will be established in your hand.
9_24_20.AMP: Now, behold, I know that you will certainly be king and that the kingdom of Israel will be established in your hand.
9_24_20.MSG: I know now beyond doubt that you will rule as king. The kingdom of Israel is already in your grasp!
9_24_20.NIV: I know that you will surely be king and that the kingdom of Israel will be established in your hands.
9_24_20.NRSVCE: Now I know that you shall surely be king, and that the kingdom of Israel shall be established in your hand.

9_24_21.NKJV: Therefore swear now to me by the LORD that you will not cut off my descendants after me, and that you will not destroy my name from my father’s house.”
9_24_21.KJV: Swear now therefore unto me by the LORD, that thou wilt not cut off my seed after me, and that thou wilt not destroy my name out of my father's house.
9_24_21.ESV: Swear to me therefore by the LORD that you will not cut off my offspring after me, and that you will not destroy my name out of my father's house.”
9_24_21.NASB: 'So now swear to me by the LORD that you will not cut off my descendants after me and that you will not destroy my name from my father's household.'
9_24_21.NLT: Now swear to me by the LORD that when that happens you will not kill my family and destroy my line of descendants!”
9_24_21.WEB: Swear now therefore to me by Yahweh, that you will not cut off my offspring after me, and that you will not destroy my name out of my father’s house.”
9_24_21.CSB17: Therefore swear to me by the LORD that you will not cut off my descendants or wipe out my name from my father’s family.”
9_24_21.AMP: So now swear to me by the LORD that you will not cut off my descendants after me and that you will not destroy my name from my father's household (extended family)."
9_24_21.MSG: Now promise me under God that you will not kill off my family or wipe my name off the books."
9_24_21.NIV: Now swear to me by the Lord that you will not cut off my descendants or wipe out my name from my father’s family.”
9_24_21.NRSVCE: Swear to me therefore by the LORD that you will not cut off my descendants after me, and that you will not wipe out my name from my father’s house.”

9_24_22.NKJV: So David swore to Saul. And Saul went home, but David and his men went up to the stronghold.
9_24_22.KJV: And David sware unto Saul. And Saul went home; but David and his men gat them up unto the hold.
9_24_22.ESV: And David swore this to Saul. Then Saul went home, but David and his men went up to the stronghold.
9_24_22.NASB: David swore to Saul. And Saul went to his home, but David and his men went up to the stronghold.
9_24_22.NLT: So David promised this to Saul with an oath. Then Saul went home, but David and his men went back to their stronghold.
9_24_22.WEB: David swore to Saul. Saul went home, but David and his men went up to the stronghold.
9_24_22.CSB17: So David swore to Saul. Then Saul went back home, and David and his men went up to the stronghold.
9_24_22.AMP: David gave Saul his oath; and Saul went home, but David and his men went up to the mountain stronghold.
9_24_22.MSG: David promised Saul. Then Saul went home and David and his men went up to their wilderness refuge.
9_24_22.NIV: So David gave his oath to Saul. Then Saul returned home, but David and his men went up to the stronghold.
9_24_22.NRSVCE: So David swore this to Saul. Then Saul went home; but David and his men went up to the stronghold.

9_25_1.NKJV: Then Samuel died; and the Israelites gathered together and lamented for him, and buried him at his home in Ramah. And David arose and went down to the Wilderness of Paran.
9_25_1.KJV: And Samuel died; and all the Israelites were gathered together, and lamented him, and buried him in his house at Ramah. And David arose, and went down to the wilderness of Paran.
9_25_1.ESV: Now Samuel died. And all Israel assembled and mourned for him, and they buried him in his house at Ramah. David and Abigail Then David rose and went down to the wilderness of Paran.
9_25_1.NASB: Then Samuel died; and all Israel gathered together and mourned for him, and buried him at his house in Ramah. And David arose and went down to the wilderness of Paran.
9_25_1.NLT: Now Samuel died, and all Israel gathered for his funeral. They buried him at his house in Ramah. Nabal Angers David Then David moved down to the wilderness of Maon.
9_25_1.WEB: Samuel died; and all Israel gathered themselves together, and mourned for him, and buried him at his house at Ramah. Then David arose, and went down to the wilderness of Paran.
9_25_1.CSB17: Samuel died, and all Israel assembled to mourn for him, and they buried him by his home in Ramah. David then went down to the Wilderness of Paran.
9_25_1.AMP: Now Samuel died; and all Israel assembled and mourned for him, and they buried him at his house in Ramah. Then David left and went down to the Wilderness of Paran.
9_25_1.MSG: Samuel died. The whole country came to his funeral. Everyone grieved over his death, and he was buried in his hometown of Ramah. Meanwhile, David moved again, this time to the wilderness of Maon.
9_25_1.NIV: David, Nabal and AbigailNow Samuel died, and all Israel assembled and mourned for him; and they buried him at his home in Ramah.Then David moved down into the Desert of Maon.
9_25_1.NRSVCE: Now Samuel died; and all Israel assembled and mourned for him. They buried him at his home in Ramah. Then David got up and went down to the wilderness of Paran.

9_25_2.NKJV: Now there was a man in Maon whose business was in Carmel, and the man was very rich. He had three thousand sheep and a thousand goats. And he was shearing his sheep in Carmel.
9_25_2.KJV: And there was a man in Maon, whose possessions were in Carmel; and the man was very great, and he had three thousand sheep, and a thousand goats: and he was shearing his sheep in Carmel. possessions: or, business
9_25_2.ESV: And there was a man in Maon whose business was in Carmel. The man was very rich; he had three thousand sheep and a thousand goats. He was shearing his sheep in Carmel.
9_25_2.NASB: Now [there was] a man in Maon whose business was in Carmel; and the man was very rich, and he had three thousand sheep and a thousand goats. And it came about while he was shearing his sheep in Carmel
9_25_2.NLT: There was a wealthy man from Maon who owned property near the town of Carmel. He had 3,000 sheep and 1,000 goats, and it was sheep-shearing time.
9_25_2.WEB: There was a man in Maon, whose possessions were in Carmel; and the man was very great. He had three thousand sheep and a thousand goats; and he was shearing his sheep in Carmel.
9_25_2.CSB17: A man in Maon had a business in Carmel; he was a very rich man with three thousand sheep and one thousand goats and was shearing his sheep in Carmel.
9_25_2.AMP: Now there was a man in Maon whose business and possessions were in Carmel; and the man was very rich. He had three thousand sheep and a thousand goats, and he was shearing his sheep in Carmel
9_25_2.MSG: There was a certain man in Maon who carried on his business in the region of Carmel. He was very prosperous—three thousand sheep and a thousand goats, and it was sheep-shearing time in Carmel.
9_25_2.NIV: A certain man in Maon, who had property there at Carmel, was very wealthy. He had a thousand goats and three thousand sheep, which he was shearing in Carmel.
9_25_2.NRSVCE: There was a man in Maon, whose property was in Carmel. The man was very rich; he had three thousand sheep and a thousand goats. He was shearing his sheep in Carmel.

9_25_3.NKJV: The name of the man was Nabal, and the name of his wife Abigail. And she was a woman of good understanding and beautiful appearance; but the man was harsh and evil in his doings. He was of the house of Caleb.
9_25_3.KJV: Now the name of the man was Nabal; and the name of his wife Abigail: and she was a woman of good understanding, and of a beautiful countenance: but the man was churlish and evil in his doings; and he was of the house of Caleb.
9_25_3.ESV: Now the name of the man was Nabal, and the name of his wife Abigail. The woman was discerning and beautiful, but the man was harsh and badly behaved; he was a Calebite.
9_25_3.NASB: (now the man's name was Nabal, and his wife's name was Abigail. And the woman was intelligent and beautiful in appearance, but the man was harsh and evil in [his] dealings, and he was a Calebite),
9_25_3.NLT: This man’s name was Nabal, and his wife, Abigail, was a sensible and beautiful woman. But Nabal, a descendant of Caleb, was crude and mean in all his dealings.
9_25_3.WEB: Now the name of the man was Nabal; and the name of his wife Abigail. This woman was intelligent and had a beautiful face; but the man was surly and evil in his doings. He was of the house of Caleb.
9_25_3.CSB17: The man’s name was Nabal, and his wife’s name, Abigail. The woman was intelligent and beautiful, but the man, a Calebite, was harsh and evil in his dealings.
9_25_3.AMP: (now the man's name was Nabal and his wife's name was Abigail. She was intelligent and beautiful in appearance, but the man was harsh and evil in his dealings; he was a Calebite).
9_25_3.MSG: The man's name was Nabal (Fool), a Calebite, and his wife's name was Abigail. The woman was intelligent and good-looking, the man brutish and mean.
9_25_3.NIV: His name was Nabal and his wife’s name was Abigail. She was an intelligent and beautiful woman, but her husband, a Calebite, was surly and mean in his dealings.
9_25_3.NRSVCE: Now the name of the man was Nabal, and the name of his wife Abigail. The woman was clever and beautiful, but the man was surly and mean; he was a Calebite.

9_25_4.NKJV: When David heard in the wilderness that Nabal was shearing his sheep,
9_25_4.KJV: And David heard in the wilderness that Nabal did shear his sheep.
9_25_4.ESV: David heard in the wilderness that Nabal was shearing his sheep.
9_25_4.NASB: that David heard in the wilderness that Nabal was shearing his sheep.
9_25_4.NLT: When David heard that Nabal was shearing his sheep,
9_25_4.WEB: David heard in the wilderness that Nabal was shearing his sheep.
9_25_4.CSB17: While David was in the wilderness, he heard that Nabal was shearing sheep,
9_25_4.AMP: David heard in the wilderness that Nabal was shearing his sheep.
9_25_4.MSG: David, out in the backcountry, heard that Nabal was shearing his sheep
9_25_4.NIV: While David was in the desert, he heard that Nabal was shearing sheep.
9_25_4.NRSVCE: David heard in the wilderness that Nabal was shearing his sheep.

9_25_5.NKJV: David sent ten young men; and David said to the young men, “Go up to Carmel, go to Nabal, and greet him in my name.
9_25_5.KJV: And David sent out ten young men, and David said unto the young men, Get you up to Carmel, and go to Nabal, and greet him in my name: greet: Heb. ask him in my name of peace
9_25_5.ESV: So David sent ten young men. And David said to the young men, “Go up to Carmel, and go to Nabal and greet him in my name.
9_25_5.NASB: So David sent ten young men; and David said to the young men, 'Go up to Carmel, visit Nabal and greet him in my name;
9_25_5.NLT: he sent ten of his young men to Carmel with this message for Nabal:
9_25_5.WEB: David sent ten young men, and David said to the young men, “Go up to Carmel, and go to Nabal, and greet him in my name.
9_25_5.CSB17: so David sent ten young men instructing them, “Go up to Carmel, and when you come to Nabal, greet him in my name.
9_25_5.AMP: So David sent ten young men; and David said to the young men, "Go up to Carmel and go to Nabal, and greet him in my name;
9_25_5.MSG: and sent ten of his young men off with these instructions: "Go to Carmel and approach Nabal. Greet him in my name, 'Peace!
9_25_5.NIV: So he sent ten young men and said to them, “Go up to Nabal at Carmel and greet him in my name.
9_25_5.NRSVCE: So David sent ten young men; and David said to the young men, “Go up to Carmel, and go to Nabal, and greet him in my name.

9_25_6.NKJV: And thus you shall say to him who lives in prosperity: ‘Peace be to you, peace to your house, and peace to all that you have!
9_25_6.KJV: And thus shall ye say to him that liveth in prosperity, Peace be both to thee, and peace be to thine house, and peace be unto all that thou hast.
9_25_6.ESV: And thus you shall greet him: ‘Peace be to you, and peace be to your house, and peace be to all that you have.
9_25_6.NASB: and thus you shall say, 'Have a long life, peace be to you, and peace be to your house, and peace be to all that you have.
9_25_6.NLT: “Peace and prosperity to you, your family, and everything you own!
9_25_6.WEB: Tell him, ‘Long life to you! Peace be to you, and peace be to your house, and peace be to all that you have.
9_25_6.CSB17: Then say this: ‘Long life to you, and peace to you, peace to your family, and peace to all that is yours.

9_25_7.NKJV: Now I have heard that you have shearers. Your shepherds were with us, and we did not hurt them, nor was there anything missing from them all the while they were in Carmel.

9_25_8.NKJV: Ask your young men, and they will tell you. Therefore let my young men find favor in your eyes, for we come on a feast day. Please give whatever comes to your hand to your servants and to your son David.’ ”

9_25_8.MSG: Ask your young men—they'll tell you. What I'm asking is that you be generous with my men—share the feast! Give whatever your heart tells you to your servants and to me, David your son.' "

9_25_21.NIV: David had just said, “It’s been useless—all my watching over this fellow’s property in the desert so that nothing of his was missing. He has paid me back evil for good.

9_25_24.NKJV: So she fell at his feet and said: “On me, my lord, on me let this iniquity be! And please let your maidservant speak in your ears, and hear the words of your maidservant.

9_25_24.ESV: She fell at his feet and said, “On me alone, my lord, be the guilt. Please let your servant speak in your ears, and hear the words of your servant.
9_25_24.NASB: She fell at his feet and said, 'On me alone, my lord, be the blame. And please let your maidservant speak to you, and listen to the words of your maidservant.
9_25_24.NLT: She fell at his feet and said, “I accept all blame in this matter, my lord. Please listen to what I have to say.

9_25_24.AMP: Kneeling at his feet she said, "My lord, let the blame and guilt be on me alone. And please let your maidservant speak to you, and listen to the words of your maidservant.

9_25_25.NKJV: Please, let not my lord regard this scoundrel Nabal. For as his name is, so is he: Nabal is his name, and folly is with him! But I, your maidservant, did not see the young men of my lord whom you sent.
9_25_25.KJV: Let not my lord, I pray thee, regard this man of Belial, even Nabal: for as his name is, so is he; Nabal is his name, and folly is with him: but I thine handmaid saw not the young men of my lord, whom thou didst send. regard: Heb. lay it to his heart Nabal: that is, Fool
9_25_25.ESV: Let not my lord regard this worthless fellow, Nabal, for as his name is, so is he. Nabal is his name, and folly is with him. But I your servant did not see the young men of my lord, whom you sent.
9_25_25.NASB: 'Please do not let my lord pay attention to this worthless man, Nabal, for as his name is, so is he. Nabal is his name and folly is with him; but I your maidservant did not see the young men of my lord whom you sent.
9_25_25.NLT: I know Nabal is a wicked and ill-tempered man; please don’t pay any attention to him. He is a fool, just as his name suggests. But I never even saw the young men you sent.
9_25_25.WEB: Please don’t let my lord pay attention to this worthless fellow, Nabal; for as his name is, so is he. Nabal is his name, and folly is with him; but I, your servant, didn’t see my lord’s young men, whom you sent.
9_25_25.CSB17: My lord should pay no attention to this worthless fool Nabal, for he lives up to his name: His name means ‘stupid,’ and stupidity is all he knows. I, your servant, didn’t see my lord’s young men whom you sent.
9_25_25.AMP: Please do not let my lord pay attention to this worthless man, Nabal, for as his name is, so is he. Nabal (fool) is his name and foolishness (stupidity) is with him; but I your maidservant did not see my lord's young men whom you sent.
9_25_25.MSG: Don't dwell on what that brute Nabal did. He acts out the meaning of his name: Nabal, Fool. Foolishness oozes from him."I wasn't there when the young men my master sent arrived. I didn't see them.
9_25_25.NIV: May my lord pay no attention to that wicked man Nabal. He is just like his name—his name is Fool, and folly goes with him. But as for me, your servant, I did not see the men my master sent.
9_25_25.NRSVCE: My lord, do not take seriously this ill-natured fellow, Nabal; for as his name is, so is he; Nabal is his name, and folly is with him; but I, your servant, did not see the young men of my lord, whom you sent.

9_25_26.NKJV: Now therefore, my lord, as the LORD lives and as your soul lives, since the LORD has held you back from coming to bloodshed and from avenging yourself with your own hand, now then, let your enemies and those who seek harm for my lord be as Nabal.

9_25_26.ESV: Now then, my lord, as the LORD lives, and as your soul lives, because the LORD has restrained you from bloodguilt and from saving with your own hand, now then let your enemies and those who seek to do evil to my lord be as Nabal.

9_25_26.AMP: So now, my lord, as the LORD lives, and as your soul lives, since the LORD has prevented you from shedding blood, and from avenging yourself by your own hand, now then let your enemies and those who seek to do evil to my lord, be as [self-destructive as] Nabal.
9_25_26.MSG: And now, my master, as God lives and as you live, God has kept you from this avenging murder—and may your enemies, all who seek my master's harm, end up like Nabal!
9_25_26.NIV: “Now since the Lord has kept you, my master, from bloodshed and from avenging yourself with your own hands, as surely as the Lord lives and as you live, may your enemies and all who intend to harm my master be like Nabal.
9_25_26.NRSVCE: “Now then, my lord, as the LORD lives, and as you yourself live, since the LORD has restrained you from bloodguilt and from taking vengeance with your own hand, now let your enemies and those who seek to do evil to my lord be like Nabal.

9_25_36.NKJV: Now Abigail went to Nabal, and there he was, holding a feast in his house, like the feast of a king. And Nabal’s heart was merry within him, for he was very drunk; therefore she told him nothing, little or much, until morning light.

9_25_36.CSB17: Then Abigail went to Nabal, and there he was in his house, holding a feast fit for a king. Nabal’s heart was cheerful, and he was very drunk, so she didn’t say anything to him until morning light.
9_25_36.AMP: Then Abigail came to Nabal, and he was holding a feast in his house [for the shearers], like the feast of a king. And Nabal's mood was joyous because he was very drunk; so she told him nothing at all until the morning light.
9_25_36.MSG: When Abigail got home she found Nabal presiding over a huge banquet. He was in high spirits—and very, very drunk. So she didn't tell him anything of what she'd done until morning.
9_25_36.NIV: When Abigail went to Nabal, he was in the house holding a banquet like that of a king. He was in high spirits and very drunk. So she told him nothing until daybreak.
9_25_36.NRSVCE: Abigail came to Nabal; he was holding a feast in his house, like the feast of a king. Nabal’s heart was merry within him, for he was very drunk; so she told him nothing at all until the morning light.

9_25_37.NKJV: So it was, in the morning, when the wine had gone from Nabal, and his wife had told him these things, that his heart died within him, and he became like a stone.

9_25_37.NASB: But in the morning, when the wine had gone out of Nabal, his wife told him these things, and his heart died within him so that he became [as] a stone.
9_25_37.NLT: In the morning when Nabal was sober, his wife told him what had happened. As a result he had a stroke, and he lay paralyzed on his bed like a stone.
9_25_37.WEB: In the morning, when the wine had gone out of Nabal, his wife told him these things, and his heart died within him, and he became as a stone.
9_25_37.CSB17: In the morning when Nabal sobered up, his wife told him about these events. His heart died and he became a stone.
9_25_37.AMP: But in the morning, when Nabal was sober, and his wife told him these things, his heart died within him and he became [paralyzed and helpless] like a stone.
9_25_37.MSG: But in the morning, after Nabal had sobered up, she told him the whole story. Right then and there he had a heart attack and fell into a coma.
9_25_37.NIV: Then in the morning, when Nabal was sober, his wife told him all these things, and his heart failed him and he became like a stone.
9_25_37.NRSVCE: In the morning, when the wine had gone out of Nabal, his wife told him these things, and his heart died within him; he became like a stone.
